The '''2011 Syrian protests''' are a series of minor protests happening in [[Syria]] which began on 26 January 2011 and are [[Tunisia Effect|influenced]] by [[2010–2011 Middle East and North Africa protests|other protests in the region]].

==Background==
On 31 January 2011, the ''[[Wall Street Journal]]'' ran an exclusive interview with [[Bashar al-Assad]], [[President]] of [[Syria]], in which he said it's time to reform. In a comment on the protests in [[Protests in Egypt|Egypt]], [[2010–2011 Tunisian uprising|Tunisia]] and [[2011 Yemeni protests|Yemen]], he said a "new era" is coming to the [[Middle East]], and that Arab rulers would need to do more to accommodate their people's rising political and economic aspirations.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052748703833204576114712441122894.html |title=Interview With Syrian President Bashar al-Assad |publisher=wsj.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-31}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052748704832704576114340735033236.html |title=Syria Strongman: Time for 'Reform' |publisher=wsj.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-31}}</ref> However, president [[Bashar al-Assad|Al-Assad]] has also declared that his state is immune from the kinds of mass protests that have taken place in Egypt.<ref>[http://www.foxnews.com/opinion/2011/02/02/middle-east-protests-syria-egypt/ FOX News opinion].</ref>

== Protests ==
=== Timeline ===
==== 26–28 January ====
On 26 January 2011, Hasan Ali Akleh from [[Al-Hasakah]] poured gasoline and burned himself in the same way as Mohamed Bouazizi. According to eyewitnesses, the action was "a protest against the [[Syrian government]]".<ref name="Hasan Ali Akleh 1">{{cite web|url=http://www.free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37797 |title=Information on the death of a young man who burned himself in Al Hasakah |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref><ref name="Hasan Ali Akleh 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.metransparent.com/spip.php?page=article&id_article=12661&lang=ar |title=Syrian suicider is "Hasan Ali Akleh". Damascus has banned a demonstration in support of Egypt |publisher=metransparent.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref> 

On 28 January 2011, a demonstration was held in [[Ar-Raqqah]] during the evening, in protest against the killing of two soldiers of [[Kurdish people|Kurdish]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37803 |title=Demonstration in Ar-Raqqa, Syria |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref>

==== 2 February ====
On 2 February, [[Human Rights Watch]] reported a group of 20 people in civilian clothing had beaten and dispersed 15 people who had been holding a candlelight vigil at [[Bab Tuma]] in Damascus for [[2011 Egyptian revolution| Egyptian demonstrators]].<ref name=guardian240211> {{cite news| url=http://www.guardian.co.uk/world/2011/feb/24/syria-crackdown-protest-arrests-beatings| title=Syria clamps down on dissent with beatings and arrests| publisher=The Guardian| date=24 February 2011| accessdate=24 February 2011 }} </ref>

==== 3–16 February ====

On 3 February another "Day of rage"  was called for in Syria from 4–5 February on [[social media]] sites Facebook and Twitter. Protesters demand reform in the government, however most protests so far have remained outside of Syria and have been small.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.msnbc.msn.com/id/41400687/ns/world_news-mideastn_africa/ |title='Day of rage' protest urged in Syria - World news - Mideast/N. Africa - msnbc.com |publisher=MSNBC |date= |accessdate=2011-02-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aysor.am/en/news/2011/02/03/syrian-facebook-twitter/ |title=“Day of Rage” planned for Syria; protests scheduled for Feb 4-5 - aysor.am - Hot news from Armenia |publisher=aysor.am |date= |accessdate=2011-02-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://freedomist.com/2011/01/30/syran-anger-day-planned-feb-5-2011/|title=Syrian Anger Day Planned Feb 5, 2011|author=William R Collier jr|date=January 30, 2011|publisher=The Freedomist|accessdate=January 31, 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://topsy.com/freedomist.com/2011/01/30/syran-anger-day-planned-feb-5-2011/ |title=Syrian Anger Day Planned Feb 5, 2011 | The Freedomist |publisher=TOPSY |date= |accessdate=2011-02-01}}</ref> The protests were expected to begin on 4 February 2011 as [[social media]] mobilised the people of [[Syria]] for rallies demanding freedom, [[human rights]] and the end to the [[state of emergency]]. Protests were also scheduled for 5 February in front of the parliament in [[Damascus]] and at Syrian embassies internationally.<ref>[http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/02/201122171649677912.html "Calls for weekend protests in Syria"].</ref> 

The only known action took place February 5, where hundreds of protesters in [[Al-Hasakah]] participated in a mass demonstration, calling for the [[Bashar al-Assad| President]]'s departure. Syrian authorities have arrested dozens and the demonstration was triggered quickly.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37849 |title=Demonstration on the day of anger in Hasaka and Syrian authorities arrested dozens |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-15}}</ref> Suhair Atassi, who runs the banned [[Jamal al-Atassi| Jamal Atassi Forum]], called for political reforms and the reinstatement of civil rights, as well as an end to the emergency law in place since 1963.<ref>{{cite magazine|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/2011/02/201129135657367367.html |title=Q&A: Syrian activist Suhair Atassi |publisher=Al Jazeera English |date=2011-02-09 |accessdate=2011-02-13}}</ref> However, no protests occurred on either date.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.upi.com/Top_News/Special/2011/02/04/Syria-quiet-on-day-of-rage/UPI-58781296853501/ |title=Syria quiet on day of rage |publisher=UPI.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.irishtimes.com/newspaper/world/2011/0205/1224289070429.html |title=Call for Syrian 'day of rage' as planned protests fail to ignite - The Irish Times - Sat, Feb 05, 2011 |publisher=The Irish Times |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Karam |first=Zeina |url=http://www.miamiherald.com/2011/02/05/2052033/syria-escapes-day-of-rage-protests.html |title=Syria escapes 'Day of Rage' protests - World Wires |publisher=MiamiHerald.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref> Though internet services were said to have been curbed, ''Facebook'' and ''Youtube'' were reported to have been restored on 8 February.<ref name="JENNIFER PRESTON"> {{cite news  
| last=  PRESTON
| first=  JENNIFER 
| url=  http://www.nytimes.com/2011/02/10/world/middleeast/10syria.html?_r=1
| title=  Syria Restores Access to Facebook and YouTube
| publisher=  [[The New York Times]]
| date=  9 February 2011
| accessdate= }} </ref> Suggestions were made that easing the ban could be a way to track activists.<ref>{{cite web|author=Jillian York |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/opinion/2011/02/2011212122746819907.html |title=Unblocking Syria's social media |publisher=Al Jazeera English |date= |accessdate=2011-02-13}}</ref> After the failure of attempts to a "day of rage," ''[[Al Jazeera]]'' called the country "a kingdom of silence" and identified key factors for stability within Syria as being strict security measures, the popularity of President [[Bashar al-Assad]], and fears of potential sectarian violence in the aftermath of a government ouster (akin to neighbouring [[Iraqi insurgency|Iraq]]).<ref>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/features/2011/02/201129103121562395.html |title=Syria: 'A kingdom of silence' |publisher=aljazeera.net |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref>

==== 17 February ====
{{External media
| video1 = {{YouTube|8Cwm5Ck2sSs|Protests in Damscus, Syria, February 17, 2011}}
}}
On February 17, a spontaneous demonstration took place outside [[Al-Hamidiyah Souq]] in Damascus, in protest at the police beating of a local shop owner. Several people gathered around and blocked the road, while they chanted "The [[Syrian people]] will not be humiliated". An eyewitness estimated the number to more than 1,500 demonstrators. [[Secret police]] officers arrived quickly to the scene along with several government officials and finally Syria's interior minister who dispersed the demonstrators and promised an investigation.<ref name=guardian240211/><ref name="ROBERT MACKEY"> {{cite news  
| last=  MACKEY
| first= ROBERT  
| url=  http://thelede.blogs.nytimes.com/2011/02/17/latest-updates-on-middle-east-protests-4/?partner=rss&emc=rss
| title=  Feb. 17 Updates on Middle East Protest
| publisher=  [[nytimes.com]]
| date=  February 17, 2011
| accessdate= }} </ref> The minister took the shop owner in his car and promised to investigate what had happened.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37926 |title=After the attack on a young man, beating and humiliation: the hundreds of Syrians demonstrating in Harika |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-17}}</ref> A couple of hours later, several video versions were posted on [[YouTube]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qDHLsU-ik_Y |title=Demonstration in Damscus Syria |publisher=[[YouTube]]|date= |accessdate=2011-02-17}}</ref>

==== 22–23 February ====
On February 22 , around 200 people gathered outside the Libyan embassy in [[Damascus]] to protest against the Libyan regime and wanted the ambassador to resign. Despite the peaceful message, there were nearly twice as many secret and uniformed police as protesters. The demonstrators carried placards reading "Freedom for the people" and "Down with [[Gaddafi]]". Everything took a new turn when the protesters started chanting "Traitors are those that beat their people.", which created nervousness among the security forces who immediately asked people to leave to their homes. 14 people were arrested and several more beaten by policemen. Some protesters were punched, kicked and beaten with sticks. All present had their identities recorded. Fourteen people were detained but later released.<ref name=guardian240211/>
<ref> {{cite web
| url=  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UNLUCg-TrZU&feature=player_embedded#at=20
| title= الاعتصام امام السفارة الليبية دمشق-22-شباط  Part 5
| publisher=  Youtube
| date=   2011/02/24
| accessdate =
}} </ref><ref> {{cite web
| url=  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLkLqI07erk
| title= الاعتصام امام السفارة الليبية دمشق-22-شباط  Part 8
| publisher=  Youtube
| date=   2011/02/24
| accessdate =
}} </ref>

On February 23, during a routine session of the [[Parliament of Syria| Syrian parliament]], a member proposed that harsh [[State of emergency| emergency laws]] to be reviewed. The issue was not scheduled for discussion, and it came as a surprise. A pro-government official at the session recalled it with anger. The proposal was rapidly quashed, when the speaker put the proposed review to a vote in the chamber, none of the other 249 MPs supported it.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thenational.ae/news/worldwide/middle-east/syrian-mp-calls-for-review-of-harsh-emergency-laws |title=Syrian MP calls for review of harsh emergency laws |publisher=The National |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>

==== 6–10 March ====
On March 6, a number of young boys, under 15, were arrested in [[Daraa]] because they have written infamous slogan "the people want to overthrow the regime" on the walls of the city.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/394E1B63-488A-4FF1-BD39-0CD306261FFE.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=سوريا تعتقل شبانا في درعا |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> Libyan [[Anti-Gaddafi forces]] shot down two Syrian war planes in [[Ras Lanuf]], that was later denied by Syrian officials.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.economicvoice.com/libyan-rebels-shoot-down-two-syrian-war-planes/50017136#axzz1FwTDTTfU |title=Libyan rebels shoot down two Syrian war planes |publisher=Economic Voice |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://syria-news.com/readnews.php?sy_seq=129725 |title=مصدر رسمي سوري ينفي مشاركة طيارين سوريين بالقتال في ليبيا |publisher=Syria-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.commentarymagazine.com/2011/03/06/syrian-pilots-shot-down-in-libya/
 |title=Syrian pilots shot down in Libya? |publisher=Commentary Magazine |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> Four brothers that are Syrian opposition activists went missing shortly after passing out flyers in Beirut in front of the Syrian embassy, calling for a demonstration to oppose Syria's government.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.monstersandcritics.com/news/middleeast/news/article_1624021.php/Search-on-for-Syrian-opposition-activists-missing-in-Lebanon |title=Search on for Syrian opposition activists missing in Lebanon |publisher=Middle East News |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref>[[Time (magazine)| Time]] says that the commitment can still be found among the Syrian youth, but what is needed is a starting point.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.time.com/time/world/article/0,8599,2057454,00.html |title=The Youth of Syria: The Rebels Are on Pause |publisher=Time.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> [[Ribal al-Assad]] says that it is almost time for Syria to be next [[Domino theory| domino]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/opinion/2011/03/20113482455647372.html |title=Is Syria the next domino? |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref>

On March 7, 13 Syrian political prisoners have gone on [[hunger strike]] to protest against "political detentions and oppression" in Syria. They demanded an end to political arrests, the removal of injustices and the restitution of rights that have been removed from civil and political life<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.jpost.com/Headlines/Article.aspx?id=211153 |title=Syrian prisoners launch hunger strike |publisher=Jerusalem Post |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.marxist.com/syria-prisoners-of-conscience-declare-hunger-strike.htm |title=Syria: Prisoners of Conscience in Damascus Central Prison declare hunger strike |publisher=marxist.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref>

On March 8, [[Syrian Arab News Agency| SANA]], official Syrian news agency published on its website news titled "President [[Bashar al-Assad| al-Assad]] issued a decree provides for a legislative grant amnesty for political crimes committed before the date of 2011-03-08". Three hours later, the publication was deleted.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://http://all4syria.info/content/view/40436/96/ |title=سيادة الرئيس يصدر عفواً عن الجرائم السياسية ثم يسحب بعد ثلاث ساعات ؟ |publisher=All4Syria |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>. Hours later, Syrian authorities released [[Haitham al-Maleh]], an 80-year-old former judge, one of [[Bashar al-Assad]]'s most outspoken critics, under an amnesty marking the anniversary of the [[1963 Syrian coup d'état| 1963 coup]] which brought the [[Baath Party]] to power.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12679902 |title=Syrian activist [[Haitham al-Maleh]] freed under amnesty |publisher=BBC News |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/08/us-syria-rights-idUSTRE7274LC20110308 |title=Syria frees 80-year-old former judge in amnesty |publisher=Reuters |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref>Twelve Syrian [[human rights]] organisations called on the government to scrap the [[state of emergency]] which has stifled civil society for almost 50 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5jVJSMH9FyXYejUJ-MoOUniMK6CBg?docId=CNG.08dc880232fd4c049d2f1a10f4641409.cf1 |title=12 rights groups in Syria demand end to emergency |publisher=AFP |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref>

On March 9, Former Defense Secretary [[Donald Rumsfeld]] released a video given to him by [[Saddam Hussein]] nearly two decades ago. Young Syrians are shown biting the heads off snakes and stabbing puppies to applause from [[Hafez al-Assad]], Syria's president for three decades until his death in 2000. Rumsfeld called the Syrian regime "barbaric".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://abcnews.go.com/Politics/donald-rumsfeld-video-saddam-hussein-depicts-barbaric-syrian/story?id=13087737 |title=Saddam Hussein's Gift to Donald Rumsfeld: Video of Syrian 'Atrocities' |publisher=ABC |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.seattlepi.com/politico/436740_politico50861.html |title=Rumsfeld releases Saddam video |publisher=seattlepi.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IGut9LbvMlg |title=وحشية النظام السوري وحافظ في فيديو نادر جداً صدر اليوم |publisher=Youtube |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref> At the same time, [[Bashar al-Assad]] seeked [[United Nations Human Rights Council]] seat, one week after [[Qaddafi]] was ousted. <ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.unwatch.org/site/apps/nlnet/content2.aspx?c=bdKKISNqEmG&b=1316871&ct=9134721 |title=Syria's Assad seeks UN rights council seat after Qaddafi ousted
 |publisher=unwatch.org |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref>

On March 10, Dozens of jailed Kurds in Syria of ''Yakiti party'' and the ''Democratic Union'' started a hunger strike in solidarity with rights activists who have mounted the same action in a prison near Damascus three days earlier.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5j_unWcGEmBcNfMzgi2zu5xlRNDzw?docId=CNG.b10f77bcf3bb312a68aa8c85b50004d3.8e1 |title=Jailed Kurds on Syria hunger strike: rights group |publisher=AFP |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Human Rights Watch]] reacts to the disappearance of Syrian activits in Lebanon four days earlier and fears that [[Lebanon]] is "back to doing Syria's dirty job".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hrw.org/en/news/2011/03/09/lebanon-reveal-fate-disappeared-syrians |title=Lebanon: Reveal Fate of Disappeared Syrians |publisher=HRW |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> Syrian Foreign Ministry stated that Syria is monitoring with high concern "the [[2011 Libyan uprising| tragic developments]] in the brotherly country of [[Libya]]".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=77422 |title=Syria respects Libya's Sovereignty |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> Syrian newspaper [[Al-Watan (Syria)| Al-Watan]] said that  the Syrian government have welcomed the fall of [[Mubarak]]'s regime and are might be looking forward new leadership that does not "cover for Israeli violations".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://http://www.syria-today.com/index.php/politics/14482-life-after-mubarak |title=Life After Mubarak |publisher=syria-today.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Iran| Iranian]] official said that they support Syria's right to "restore the occupied [[Golan Heights| Golan]] under its sovereignty".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=77335 |title=Iran support Syria to restore the occupied Golan |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Reform Party of Syria]] said that "[[Bashar al-Assad| al-Assad]] is sending arms to [[Gaddafi]] to kill his people with". <ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.worldtribune.com/worldtribune/WTARC/2011/me_libya0257_03_10.asp |title=Syrian pilots said to be flying Libyan fighter jets |publisher=World Tribune |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>

==== 12–13 March ====
On March 12, thousands of Syrian Kurds in [[al-Qamishli]] and [[al-Hasakah]] protested on the day of Kurdish martyr, an annual event since 2004, when many Syrian kurds died in anti-goverment demonstrations.<ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة في الجزيرة السورية 12 اذار 2011 |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7TXnYz5OsI8 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=حفلة تأبين شهداء إنتفاضة قامشلو  |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MWKh8C5Z6ps |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=الكورد السوريون يحييون ذكرى انتفاضتهم السابعة بايقاد الشموع اجلالاً و اكراماُ لارواح الشهداء |author=soparo.com |url=http://ar.soparo.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=17803%3A2011-03-11-23-36-17&catid=34%3A2008-05-23-23-58-43&Itemid=192 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> [[Al Jazeera]] reported that a civilian vessel, loaded with weapons and ammunition and 500 [[SUV]]s had sailed from the port of [[Tartus]] in Syria towards [[Tripoli]] in Libya, in order to supply [[Military of Libya| Gaddafi forces]]. The reporter said that the Syrian battalion fighting alongside [[Gaddafi]] against the [[Anti-Gaddafi forces| Libyan rebels]].<ref>{{cite news |title=النظام الاسدي الفاسد يقتل الشعب الليبي باسلحته |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j0ZfsdCXFJA |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=دعم النظام السوري لسفاح ليبيا |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qE6C7LNCRFQ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> That was also confirmed with an interview with Libyan politician Ibrahim Jibreel.<ref>{{cite news |title=الجزيرة ـ إبراهيم جبريل والتواطؤ السوري مع نظام القذافي |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZaPyJhLIVdA&feature=player_embedded#at=387
 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref>

Newly released regime critic [[Haitham al-Maleh]] announced in a Youtube video his support and assistance to the Syrian youth who are behind the new wave of protests and hoped that he will soon see democracy in Syria.<ref>{{cite news |title=هيثم المالح .. يوجه رسالة إلى شباب الثورة السورية |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EVYVtaVKnf0 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> During an emergency meeting, Syria voted against [[Arab League]]'s proposal to impose a no-fly zone over Libya.<ref>{{cite news |title=Syria voted against Libya no fly zone |author=yalibnan.com |url=http://www.yalibnan.com/2011/03/12/20757/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref>

On March 13, ''Kamal Hussein Cheikho'', Kurdish militant, blogger and a member of the Committee for the Defence of Democratic Liberties and Human Rights in Syria (CDF), was released on bail of 500 Syrian pounds ($10). He is still facing charges for allegedly publishing material harmful to the country.<ref name="OMCT">{{cite news |title=Syrian Arab Republic: Assault and arrest of dozens of family members of human rights defenders and prisoners of conscience |author=OMCT |url=http://www.omct.org/human-rights-defenders/urgent-interventions/syrian-arab-republic/2011/03/d21153/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syria blogger arrest condemned |author=http://mybroadband.co.za |url=http://mybroadband.co.za/news/internet/18663-Syria-blogger-arrest-condemned.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=24 February 2011}}</ref><ref name="ahram">{{cite news |title=Arrests as Syria sees second day of rare protests |author=ahram.org.eg |url=http://english.ahram.org.eg/NewsContent/2/8/7879/World/Region/Arrests-as-Syria-sees-second-day-of-rare-protests.aspx |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 15 March ====
Simultaneous demonstrations took place in major cities across Syria. Thousands of protestors gathered in [[Aleppo]], [[al-Hasakah]], [[Daraa]], [[Deir ez-Zor]], and [[Hama]]. There were some clashes with security, according to reports from dissident groups. In [[Damascus]], a smaller group of 200 people grew spontaneously to about 1,500 demonstrators. Damascus has not seen such protests since the 1980s. The official Facebook page called "Syrian Revolution 2011" shown pictures of supportive demonstrations in [[Cairo]], [[Nicosia]], [[Helsinki]], [[Istanbul]] and [[Berlin]]. There were also unconfirmed news that supporters of Libyan descent stormed into the Syrian Embassy in [[Paris]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Fresh Protests Erupt in Syria |author=theepochtimes.com |url=http://www.theepochtimes.com/n2/content/view/53074/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=السوريون يكسرون جدار الخوف بالتظاهر في قلب دمشق |author=Youtube/Al Arabiya |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rRHRxUxRbxo |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=ردّدوا هتافات تدعو لمحاربة الفساد وفتح باب الحريات |author=Al Arabiya |url=http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/03/15/141661.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=الاف السوريين يثورون في قلب دمشق و المحافظات مطالبين بالحرية |author=sawtbeirut.com |url=http://www.sawtbeirut.com/news-in-arabic/world-now/16474-syria-revolution-2011-march-15.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة شباب الثورة في تركيا |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7AbjW0Kuhl8 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

Another recently released political [[Suhair Atassi]] became an unofficial spokesperson for the "Syrian revolution", when she was interviewed by dozens of Arab and international media channels regarding the protests.  There were reports of 6 arrested in Damascus.<ref>{{cite news |title=آخر اخبار انتفاضة سورية من فضائية الجزيرة |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sMNBN1PhjzY |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=تقرير قناه ال BBC للانتفاضه السوريه |author=Youtube/BBC |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iAUOqfRSEaM |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة أمام السفارة السورية اليوم بالتزامن مع إنطلاق الثورة السورية |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oCA4iSugabU |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة احتجاج في دمشق تطالب بالحريات |author=BBC |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/arabic/middleeast/2011/03/110315_syria_protest.shtml |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref> Atassi paid tribute to "the Syrian people who took the initiative ahead of the opposition," recalling the popular uprisings that shook [[Tunisian Revolution| Tunisia]] and [[2011 Egyptian revolution| Egypt]]<ref name="ahram" />After the first day of the protests there were reports about approximately 3000 arrests and a few "[[martyrs| martyrs]]", but there are no official figures on the number of deaths.<ref>{{cite news |title=معلومات عن سقوط شهداء في تظاهرات الثلاثاء في سوريا |author=sawtbeirut.com |url=http://sawtbeirut.com/news-in-arabic/world-now/16483-2011-03-16-18-33-05.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 16 March ====
Syrian authorities forcibly dispersed a crowd composed of 200 demonstrators in front of the Syrian Interior Ministry. [[al-Arabiya]] reported that protesters were a mix of activists and jurists, writers, journalists, young academics and family members to people detained in Syrian prisons.<ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian Security Forces Break Up Damascus Protest |author=voanews.com |url=http://www.voanews.com/english/news/middle-east/Syrian-Security-Forces-Break-Up-Damascus-Protest-118085199.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Rare public protest quashed in Syria |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011316131236735771.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian security forces disperse Damascus demo |author=AFP |url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5hN_8F5MkEvOPFuMqr-9HStxVy3iw?docId=CNG.e314ba0dc0a9061349e6fe1072be714a.5f1 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> Several security officers managed to infiltrate themselves in demonstrations at different places and started shouting slogans declaring love and loyalty to President [[Bashar al-Assad| al-Assad]].<ref>{{cite news |title=بلطجية بشار |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s0NcdFYFR0Q |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=بلطجية بشار يفرقونا ويهتفون باسمه |author=Facebook Video |url=http://www.facebook.com/video/video.php?v=110837738996912&oid=420796315726&comments |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> The security forces arrested a number of protestors, [[Al Jazeera]] reported 25<ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة أمام القنصلية السورية في اسطنبول |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CyXg8Hm8Hjs |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>, while [[Al Arabiya]] said 32<ref>{{cite news |title= أسماء الناشطين المعتقلين من قبل قوات الأمن السورية |author=Youtube/Al Arabiya |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OJCttMTen2w |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> including activist and lawyer [[Suhair Atassi]] and Kamal Cheikho, an activist who was released two days earlier.<ref>{{cite news |title=أهالي سجناء وناشطون حقوقيون شاركوا في التجمع |author=Al Arabiya |url=http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/03/16/141801.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=اعتقالات وتفريق مظاهرة في دمشق |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/04E7BD6C-CA0E-4597-A298-0494DD715056.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=سورية: مظاهرة امام وزارة الداخلية في العاصمة دمشق |author=BBC |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/arabic/middleeast/2011/03/110316_syria_prisoners_families.shtml |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> [[World Organisation Against Torture]] published list of arrests and demanded immediate release of them.<ref name="OMCT" />

''Mohamed al-Ali'', a spokesman for the Syrian Interior authority denied that it has happened any demonstrations in Syria and that the Facebook-campaign has been proved unsuccessful. According to the spokesperson, the "claimed protests" consisted of a bunch of few people who were "hiding" among the already packed souq and tried to make it look like a demonstration.<ref>{{cite news |title=تصريح العميد العلي عن مظاهرة وزارة الداخلية |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WevqUv1gGmw |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> In another statement, he went further by saying that the demonstration which was outside the Interior authority was actually in support of President [[Bashar al-Assad]].<ref>{{cite news |title=وزارة الداخلية السورية - المظاهرات كانت لتأييد الرئيس |author=Youtube/BBC |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DXU4ascw1G0
 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref>

==Reaction==
On 29 January 2011, news channel [[Al Arabiya]] reported that Internet connection was cut off, that was quickly denied by official sources.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=72236 |title=Internet is Available across Syria |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref> 
''Al Jazeera'' reported increased security for the planned "Days of rage"<ref>{{cite web |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/features/2011/02/201129103121562395.html |title=Syria: 'A kingdom of silence' |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-02-09}}</ref> . Days before the planned protests, Syrian authorities arrested several political activists, such as businessman Ghassan al-Najar, leader of the Islamic Democratic movement,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.elaph.com/Web/news/2011/2/629499.html |title=Arrest of leader of the Islamic Democratic movement in Syria |publisher=elaph.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37876 |title=Transfer of activist Ghassan Najar to Adra prison after being referred for trial |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> the writer Ali al-Abdallah,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37861 |title=Start of the trial exhibits and the Syrian writer Ali Abdullah |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> Abbas Abbas, from [[Syrian Communist Party]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37788 |title=Jailed prominent Syrian opposition for seven and a half years |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> and several other political personalities of Kurdish backgrounds, such as Adnan Mustafa.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37802 |title=Syrian authorities detain national identity Adnan Mustafa Abu Ammar |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref>

On February 14, blogger and student [[Tal al-Mallohi]] was sentenced to five years in prison for spying for the United States. Washington denied these allegations and asked for the release Tal immediately. February 15, under pressure from human rights organizations, the Syrian government released ''Ghassan al-Najjar'', a "veteran Islamist activist" after he went on hunger strike following his arrest for calling for mass protests.<ref>{{cite news | url = http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/02/16/us-protests-idUSTRE71F41K20110216?pageNumber=2 | title = Egypt-inspired protests gain pace across region | first = Paul | last = Taylor | date = 2011-02-16 | publisher = Reuters }}</ref> February 16, regime critic and director of the Organisation for Democracy and Freedom in Syria (ODFS) [[Ribal al-Assad]], the son of [[Rifaat al-Assad]] and cousin to Syrian President [[Bashar al-Assad]] held a press conference in London, where he made it clear that he "does not want to see a Syrian revolution, but a peaceful change of power".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.elaph.com/Web/news/2011/2/632409.html |title=Opposition leader Ribal Al-Assad is optimistic to changes in Syria |publisher=elaph.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-16}}</ref>

Under pressure from Internet activists that [[Al Jazeera]] has not acknowledged the Syrian protests, special coverage was released on [[Hama massacre]] as well as analysis of the largest [[opposition parties]] in Syria which might have great political influence in any change of power, [[Syrian Communist Party]], [[Muslim Brotherhood]], [[National Salvation Front in Syria| National Salvation Front]], [[Movement for Justice and Development in Syria| Movement for Justice and Development]], [[Reform Party of Syria| Reform Party]], [[Arab Socialist Movement]], [[Arab Socialist Union (Syria)| Arab Socialist Union]], [[Workers Revolutionary Party]], [[Communist party (disambiguation)#Labor.2C Labour| Communist Labour Party]] among others.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gKzsLSDbd8E |title=الثورة السورية.. اتصال بالجزيرة فداكي ياسوريا |publisher=Youtube |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/31339303-299F-413A-8986-49DE94D1F04D.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=مجزرة حماة.. وقائع منسية |publisher=Al Jazeera|date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/605F9657-EC6C-4754-A38B-989C563CB129.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=قوى المعارضة في سوريا |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> On March 9, [[Al Jazeera]] continued its reporting with analysis of opinion detainees in Syria<ref>{{cite news |title=معتقلو الرأي في سوريا |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/0175E8AF-1D2E-4CB2-9473-8EC8528A5224.htm |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=9 March 2011}}</ref> and two days later another special report said that many activists showed their displeasure to the general decree of amnesty did not include political prisoners.<ref>{{cite news |title=ضيق لاستبعاد سياسيين من عفو سوريا |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/C6F6E23B-AC60-4F4F-B3E2-C8F3080DCD2E.htm |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=11 March 2011}}</ref>

The '''2011 Syrian protests''' are a series of protests happening in [[Syria]] which began on 26 January 2011 and are [[Tunisia Effect|influenced]] by [[2010–2011 Middle East and North Africa protests|other protests in the region]].

==Background==
On 31 January 2011, the ''[[Wall Street Journal]]'' ran an exclusive interview with [[Bashar al-Assad]], [[President]] of [[Syria]], in which he said it's time to reform. In a comment on the protests in [[Protests in Egypt|Egypt]], [[2010–2011 Tunisian uprising|Tunisia]] and [[2011 Yemeni protests|Yemen]], he said a "new era" is coming to the [[Middle East]], and that Arab rulers would need to do more to accommodate their people's rising political and economic aspirations.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052748703833204576114712441122894.html |title=Interview With Syrian President Bashar al-Assad |publisher=wsj.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-31}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052748704832704576114340735033236.html |title=Syria Strongman: Time for 'Reform' |publisher=wsj.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-31}}</ref> However, president [[Bashar al-Assad|Al-Assad]] has also declared that his state is immune from the kinds of mass protests that have taken place in Egypt.<ref>[http://www.foxnews.com/opinion/2011/02/02/middle-east-protests-syria-egypt/ FOX News opinion].</ref>

== Protests ==
=== Timeline ===
==== 26–28 January ====
On 26 January 2011, Hasan Ali Akleh from [[Al-Hasakah]] poured gasoline and burned himself in the same way as Mohamed Bouazizi. According to eyewitnesses, the action was "a protest against the [[Syrian government]]".<ref name="Hasan Ali Akleh 1">{{cite web|url=http://www.free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37797 |title=Information on the death of a young man who burned himself in Al Hasakah |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref><ref name="Hasan Ali Akleh 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.metransparent.com/spip.php?page=article&id_article=12661&lang=ar |title=Syrian suicider is "Hasan Ali Akleh". Damascus has banned a demonstration in support of Egypt |publisher=metransparent.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref> 

On 28 January 2011, a demonstration was held in [[Ar-Raqqah]] during the evening, in protest against the killing of two soldiers of [[Kurdish people|Kurdish]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37803 |title=Demonstration in Ar-Raqqa, Syria |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref>

==== 2 February ====
On 2 February, [[Human Rights Watch]] reported a group of 20 people in civilian clothing had beaten and dispersed 15 people who had been holding a candlelight vigil at [[Bab Tuma]] in Damascus for [[2011 Egyptian revolution| Egyptian demonstrators]].<ref name=guardian240211> {{cite news| url=http://www.guardian.co.uk/world/2011/feb/24/syria-crackdown-protest-arrests-beatings| title=Syria clamps down on dissent with beatings and arrests| publisher=The Guardian| date=24 February 2011| accessdate=24 February 2011 }} </ref>

==== 3–16 February ====

On 3 February another "Day of rage"  was called for in Syria from 4–5 February on [[social media]] sites Facebook and Twitter. Protesters demand reform in the government, however most protests so far have remained outside of Syria and have been small.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.msnbc.msn.com/id/41400687/ns/world_news-mideastn_africa/ |title='Day of rage' protest urged in Syria - World news - Mideast/N. Africa - msnbc.com |publisher=MSNBC |date= |accessdate=2011-02-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aysor.am/en/news/2011/02/03/syrian-facebook-twitter/ |title=“Day of Rage” planned for Syria; protests scheduled for Feb 4-5 - aysor.am - Hot news from Armenia |publisher=aysor.am |date= |accessdate=2011-02-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://freedomist.com/2011/01/30/syran-anger-day-planned-feb-5-2011/|title=Syrian Anger Day Planned Feb 5, 2011|author=William R Collier jr|date=January 30, 2011|publisher=The Freedomist|accessdate=January 31, 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://topsy.com/freedomist.com/2011/01/30/syran-anger-day-planned-feb-5-2011/ |title=Syrian Anger Day Planned Feb 5, 2011 | The Freedomist |publisher=TOPSY |date= |accessdate=2011-02-01}}</ref> The protests were expected to begin on 4 February 2011 as [[social media]] mobilised the people of [[Syria]] for rallies demanding freedom, [[human rights]] and the end to the [[state of emergency]]. Protests were also scheduled for 5 February in front of the parliament in [[Damascus]] and at Syrian embassies internationally.<ref>[http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/02/201122171649677912.html "Calls for weekend protests in Syria"].</ref> 

The only known action took place February 5, where hundreds of protesters in [[Al-Hasakah]] participated in a mass demonstration, calling for the [[Bashar al-Assad| President]]'s departure. Syrian authorities have arrested dozens and the demonstration was triggered quickly.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37849 |title=Demonstration on the day of anger in Hasaka and Syrian authorities arrested dozens |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-15}}</ref> Suhair Atassi, who runs the banned [[Jamal al-Atassi| Jamal Atassi Forum]], called for political reforms and the reinstatement of civil rights, as well as an end to the emergency law in place since 1963.<ref>{{cite magazine|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/2011/02/201129135657367367.html |title=Q&A: Syrian activist Suhair Atassi |publisher=Al Jazeera English |date=2011-02-09 |accessdate=2011-02-13}}</ref> However, no protests occurred on either date.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.upi.com/Top_News/Special/2011/02/04/Syria-quiet-on-day-of-rage/UPI-58781296853501/ |title=Syria quiet on day of rage |publisher=UPI.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.irishtimes.com/newspaper/world/2011/0205/1224289070429.html |title=Call for Syrian 'day of rage' as planned protests fail to ignite - The Irish Times - Sat, Feb 05, 2011 |publisher=The Irish Times |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Karam |first=Zeina |url=http://www.miamiherald.com/2011/02/05/2052033/syria-escapes-day-of-rage-protests.html |title=Syria escapes 'Day of Rage' protests - World Wires |publisher=MiamiHerald.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref> Though internet services were said to have been curbed, ''Facebook'' and ''Youtube'' were reported to have been restored on 8 February.<ref name="JENNIFER PRESTON"> {{cite news  
| last=  PRESTON
| first=  JENNIFER 
| url=  http://www.nytimes.com/2011/02/10/world/middleeast/10syria.html?_r=1
| title=  Syria Restores Access to Facebook and YouTube
| publisher=  [[The New York Times]]
| date=  9 February 2011
| accessdate= }} </ref> Suggestions were made that easing the ban could be a way to track activists.<ref>{{cite web|author=Jillian York |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/opinion/2011/02/2011212122746819907.html |title=Unblocking Syria's social media |publisher=Al Jazeera English |date= |accessdate=2011-02-13}}</ref> After the failure of attempts to a "day of rage," ''[[Al Jazeera]]'' called the country "a kingdom of silence" and identified key factors for stability within Syria as being strict security measures, the popularity of President [[Bashar al-Assad]], and fears of potential sectarian violence in the aftermath of a government ouster (akin to neighbouring [[Iraqi insurgency|Iraq]]).<ref>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/features/2011/02/201129103121562395.html |title=Syria: 'A kingdom of silence' |publisher=aljazeera.net |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref>

==== 17 February ====
{{External media
| video1 = {{YouTube|8Cwm5Ck2sSs|Protests in Damscus, Syria, February 17, 2011}}
}}
On February 17, a spontaneous demonstration took place outside [[Al-Hamidiyah Souq]] in Damascus, in protest at the police beating of a local shop owner. Several people gathered around and blocked the road, while they chanted "The [[Syrian people]] will not be humiliated". An eyewitness estimated the number to more than 1,500 demonstrators. [[Secret police]] officers arrived quickly to the scene along with several government officials and finally Syria's interior minister who dispersed the demonstrators and promised an investigation.<ref name=guardian240211/><ref name="ROBERT MACKEY"> {{cite news  
| last=  MACKEY
| first= ROBERT  
| url=  http://thelede.blogs.nytimes.com/2011/02/17/latest-updates-on-middle-east-protests-4/?partner=rss&emc=rss
| title=  Feb. 17 Updates on Middle East Protest
| publisher=  [[nytimes.com]]
| date=  February 17, 2011
| accessdate= }} </ref> The minister took the shop owner in his car and promised to investigate what had happened.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37926 |title=After the attack on a young man, beating and humiliation: the hundreds of Syrians demonstrating in Harika |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-17}}</ref> A couple of hours later, several video versions were posted on [[YouTube]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qDHLsU-ik_Y |title=Demonstration in Damscus Syria |publisher=[[YouTube]]|date= |accessdate=2011-02-17}}</ref>

==== 22–23 February ====
On February 22 , around 200 people gathered outside the Libyan embassy in [[Damascus]] to protest against the Libyan regime and wanted the ambassador to resign. Despite the peaceful message, there were nearly twice as many secret and uniformed police as protesters. The demonstrators carried placards reading "Freedom for the people" and "Down with [[Gaddafi]]". Everything took a new turn when the protesters started chanting "Traitors are those that beat their people.", which created nervousness among the security forces who immediately asked people to leave to their homes. 14 people were arrested and several more beaten by policemen. Some protesters were punched, kicked and beaten with sticks. All present had their identities recorded. Fourteen people were detained but later released.<ref name=guardian240211/>
<ref> {{cite web
| url=  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UNLUCg-TrZU&feature=player_embedded#at=20
| title= الاعتصام امام السفارة الليبية دمشق-22-شباط  Part 5
| publisher=  Youtube
| date=   2011/02/24
| accessdate =
}} </ref><ref> {{cite web
| url=  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLkLqI07erk
| title= الاعتصام امام السفارة الليبية دمشق-22-شباط  Part 8
| publisher=  Youtube
| date=   2011/02/24

}} </ref>

On February 23, during a routine session of the [[Parliament of Syria| Syrian parliament]], a member proposed that harsh [[State of emergency| emergency laws]] to be reviewed. The issue was not scheduled for discussion, and it came as a surprise. A pro-government official at the session recalled it with anger. The proposal was rapidly quashed, when the speaker put the proposed review to a vote in the chamber, none of the other 249 MPs supported it.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thenational.ae/news/worldwide/middle-east/syrian-mp-calls-for-review-of-harsh-emergency-laws |title=Syrian MP calls for review of harsh emergency laws |publisher=The National |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>

==== 6–10 March ====
On March 6, a number of young boys, under 15, were arrested in [[Daraa]] because they have written infamous slogan "the people want to overthrow the regime" on the walls of the city.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/394E1B63-488A-4FF1-BD39-0CD306261FFE.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=سوريا تعتقل شبانا في درعا |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> Libyan [[Anti-Gaddafi forces]] shot down two Syrian war planes in [[Ras Lanuf]], that was later denied by Syrian officials.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.economicvoice.com/libyan-rebels-shoot-down-two-syrian-war-planes/50017136#axzz1FwTDTTfU |title=Libyan rebels shoot down two Syrian war planes |publisher=Economic Voice |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://syria-news.com/readnews.php?sy_seq=129725 |title=مصدر رسمي سوري ينفي مشاركة طيارين سوريين بالقتال في ليبيا |publisher=Syria-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.commentarymagazine.com/2011/03/06/syrian-pilots-shot-down-in-libya/
 |title=Syrian pilots shot down in Libya? |publisher=Commentary Magazine |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> Four brothers that are Syrian opposition activists went missing shortly after passing out flyers in Beirut in front of the Syrian embassy, calling for a demonstration to oppose Syria's government.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.monstersandcritics.com/news/middleeast/news/article_1624021.php/Search-on-for-Syrian-opposition-activists-missing-in-Lebanon |title=Search on for Syrian opposition activists missing in Lebanon |publisher=Middle East News |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref>[[Time (magazine)| Time]] says that the commitment can still be found among the Syrian youth, but what is needed is a starting point.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.time.com/time/world/article/0,8599,2057454,00.html |title=The Youth of Syria: The Rebels Are on Pause |publisher=Time.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> [[Ribal al-Assad]] says that it is almost time for Syria to be next [[Domino theory| domino]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/opinion/2011/03/20113482455647372.html |title=Is Syria the next domino? |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref>

On March 7, 13 Syrian political prisoners have gone on [[hunger strike]] to protest against "political detentions and oppression" in Syria. They demanded an end to political arrests, the removal of injustices and the restitution of rights that have been removed from civil and political life<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.jpost.com/Headlines/Article.aspx?id=211153 |title=Syrian prisoners launch hunger strike |publisher=Jerusalem Post |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.marxist.com/syria-prisoners-of-conscience-declare-hunger-strike.htm |title=Syria: Prisoners of Conscience in Damascus Central Prison declare hunger strike |publisher=marxist.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref>

On March 8, [[Syrian Arab News Agency| SANA]], official Syrian news agency published on its website news titled "President [[Bashar al-Assad| al-Assad]] issued a decree provides for a legislative grant amnesty for political crimes committed before the date of 2011-03-08". Three hours later, the publication was deleted.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://http://all4syria.info/content/view/40436/96/ |title=سيادة الرئيس يصدر عفواً عن الجرائم السياسية ثم يسحب بعد ثلاث ساعات ؟ |publisher=All4Syria |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>. Hours later, Syrian authorities released [[Haitham al-Maleh]], an 80-year-old former judge, one of [[Bashar al-Assad]]'s most outspoken critics, under an amnesty marking the anniversary of the [[1963 Syrian coup d'état| 1963 coup]] which brought the [[Baath Party]] to power.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12679902 |title=Syrian activist [[Haitham al-Maleh]] freed under amnesty |publisher=BBC News |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/08/us-syria-rights-idUSTRE7274LC20110308 |title=Syria frees 80-year-old former judge in amnesty |publisher=Reuters |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref>Twelve Syrian [[human rights]] organisations called on the government to scrap the [[state of emergency]] which has stifled civil society for almost 50 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5jVJSMH9FyXYejUJ-MoOUniMK6CBg?docId=CNG.08dc880232fd4c049d2f1a10f4641409.cf1 |title=12 rights groups in Syria demand end to emergency |publisher=AFP |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref>

On March 9, Former Defense Secretary [[Donald Rumsfeld]] released a video given to him by [[Saddam Hussein]] nearly two decades ago. Young Syrians are shown biting the heads off snakes and stabbing puppies to applause from [[Hafez al-Assad]], Syria's president for three decades until his death in 2000. Rumsfeld called the Syrian regime "barbaric".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://abcnews.go.com/Politics/donald-rumsfeld-video-saddam-hussein-depicts-barbaric-syrian/story?id=13087737 |title=Saddam Hussein's Gift to Donald Rumsfeld: Video of Syrian 'Atrocities' |publisher=ABC |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.seattlepi.com/politico/436740_politico50861.html |title=Rumsfeld releases Saddam video |publisher=seattlepi.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IGut9LbvMlg |title=وحشية النظام السوري وحافظ في فيديو نادر جداً صدر اليوم |publisher=Youtube |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref> At the same time, [[Bashar al-Assad]] seeked [[United Nations Human Rights Council]] seat, one week after [[Qaddafi]] was ousted. <ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.unwatch.org/site/apps/nlnet/content2.aspx?c=bdKKISNqEmG&b=1316871&ct=9134721 |title=Syria's Assad seeks UN rights council seat after Qaddafi ousted
 |publisher=unwatch.org |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref>

On March 10, Dozens of jailed Kurds in Syria of ''Yakiti party'' and the ''Democratic Union'' started a hunger strike in solidarity with rights activists who have mounted the same action in a prison near Damascus three days earlier.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5j_unWcGEmBcNfMzgi2zu5xlRNDzw?docId=CNG.b10f77bcf3bb312a68aa8c85b50004d3.8e1 |title=Jailed Kurds on Syria hunger strike: rights group |publisher=AFP |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Human Rights Watch]] reacts to the disappearance of Syrian activits in Lebanon four days earlier and fears that [[Lebanon]] is "back to doing Syria's dirty job".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hrw.org/en/news/2011/03/09/lebanon-reveal-fate-disappeared-syrians |title=Lebanon: Reveal Fate of Disappeared Syrians |publisher=HRW |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> Syrian Foreign Ministry stated that Syria is monitoring with high concern "the [[2011 Libyan uprising| tragic developments]] in the brotherly country of [[Libya]]".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=77422 |title=Syria respects Libya's Sovereignty |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> Syrian newspaper [[Al-Watan (Syria)| Al-Watan]] said that  the Syrian government have welcomed the fall of [[Mubarak]]'s regime and are might be looking forward new leadership that does not "cover for Israeli violations".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://http://www.syria-today.com/index.php/politics/14482-life-after-mubarak |title=Life After Mubarak |publisher=syria-today.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Iran| Iranian]] official said that they support Syria's right to "restore the occupied [[Golan Heights| Golan]] under its sovereignty".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=77335 |title=Iran support Syria to restore the occupied Golan |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Reform Party of Syria]] said that "[[Bashar al-Assad| al-Assad]] is sending arms to [[Gaddafi]] to kill his people with". <ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.worldtribune.com/worldtribune/WTARC/2011/me_libya0257_03_10.asp |title=Syrian pilots said to be flying Libyan fighter jets |publisher=World Tribune |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>

==== 12–13 March ====
On March 12, thousands of Syrian Kurds in [[al-Qamishli]] and [[al-Hasakah]] protested on the day of Kurdish martyr, an annual event since 2004, when many Syrian kurds died in anti-goverment demonstrations.<ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة في الجزيرة السورية 12 اذار 2011 |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7TXnYz5OsI8 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=حفلة تأبين شهداء إنتفاضة قامشلو  |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MWKh8C5Z6ps |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=الكورد السوريون يحييون ذكرى انتفاضتهم السابعة بايقاد الشموع اجلالاً و اكراماُ لارواح الشهداء |author=soparo.com |url=http://ar.soparo.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=17803%3A2011-03-11-23-36-17&catid=34%3A2008-05-23-23-58-43&Itemid=192 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> [[Al Jazeera]] reported that a civilian vessel, loaded with weapons and ammunition and 500 [[SUV]]s had sailed from the port of [[Tartus]] in Syria towards [[Tripoli]] in Libya, in order to supply [[Military of Libya| Gaddafi forces]]. The reporter said that the Syrian battalion fighting alongside [[Gaddafi]] against the [[Anti-Gaddafi forces| Libyan rebels]].<ref>{{cite news |title=النظام الاسدي الفاسد يقتل الشعب الليبي باسلحته |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j0ZfsdCXFJA |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=دعم النظام السوري لسفاح ليبيا |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qE6C7LNCRFQ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> That was also confirmed with an interview with Libyan politician Ibrahim Jibreel.<ref>{{cite news |title=الجزيرة ـ إبراهيم جبريل والتواطؤ السوري مع نظام القذافي |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZaPyJhLIVdA&feature=player_embedded#at=387
 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref>

Newly released regime critic [[Haitham al-Maleh]] announced in a Youtube video his support and assistance to the Syrian youth who are behind the new wave of protests and hoped that he will soon see democracy in Syria.<ref>{{cite news |title=هيثم المالح .. يوجه رسالة إلى شباب الثورة السورية |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EVYVtaVKnf0 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> During an emergency meeting, Syria voted against [[Arab League]]'s proposal to impose a no-fly zone over Libya.<ref>{{cite news |title=Syria voted against Libya no fly zone |author=yalibnan.com |url=http://www.yalibnan.com/2011/03/12/20757/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref>

On March 13, ''Kamal Hussein Cheikho'', Kurdish militant, blogger and a member of the Committee for the Defence of Democratic Liberties and Human Rights in Syria (CDF), was released on bail of 500 Syrian pounds ($10). He is still facing charges for allegedly publishing material harmful to the country.<ref name="OMCT">{{cite news |title=Syrian Arab Republic: Assault and arrest of dozens of family members of human rights defenders and prisoners of conscience |author=OMCT |url=http://www.omct.org/human-rights-defenders/urgent-interventions/syrian-arab-republic/2011/03/d21153/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syria blogger arrest condemned |author=http://mybroadband.co.za |url=http://mybroadband.co.za/news/internet/18663-Syria-blogger-arrest-condemned.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=24 February 2011}}</ref><ref name="ahram">{{cite news |title=Arrests as Syria sees second day of rare protests |author=ahram.org.eg |url=http://english.ahram.org.eg/NewsContent/2/8/7879/World/Region/Arrests-as-Syria-sees-second-day-of-rare-protests.aspx |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 15 March ====
Simultaneous demonstrations took place in major cities across Syria. Thousands of protestors gathered in [[Aleppo]], [[al-Hasakah]], [[Daraa]], [[Deir ez-Zor]], and [[Hama]]. There were some clashes with security, according to reports from dissident groups. In [[Damascus]], a smaller group of 200 people grew spontaneously to about 1,500 demonstrators. Damascus has not seen such protests since the 1980s. The official Facebook page called "Syrian Revolution 2011" shown pictures of supportive demonstrations in [[Cairo]], [[Nicosia]], [[Helsinki]], [[Istanbul]] and [[Berlin]]. There were also unconfirmed news that supporters of Libyan descent stormed into the Syrian Embassy in [[Paris]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Fresh Protests Erupt in Syria |author=theepochtimes.com |url=http://www.theepochtimes.com/n2/content/view/53074/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=السوريون يكسرون جدار الخوف بالتظاهر في قلب دمشق |author=Youtube/Al Arabiya |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rRHRxUxRbxo |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=ردّدوا هتافات تدعو لمحاربة الفساد وفتح باب الحريات |author=Al Arabiya |url=http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/03/15/141661.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=الاف السوريين يثورون في قلب دمشق و المحافظات مطالبين بالحرية |author=sawtbeirut.com |url=http://www.sawtbeirut.com/news-in-arabic/world-now/16474-syria-revolution-2011-march-15.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة شباب الثورة في تركيا |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7AbjW0Kuhl8 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

Another recently released political [[Suhair Atassi]] became an unofficial spokesperson for the "Syrian revolution", when she was interviewed by dozens of Arab and international media channels regarding the protests.  There were reports of 6 arrested in Damascus.<ref>{{cite news |title=آخر اخبار انتفاضة سورية من فضائية الجزيرة |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sMNBN1PhjzY |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=تقرير قناه ال BBC للانتفاضه السوريه |author=Youtube/BBC |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iAUOqfRSEaM |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة أمام السفارة السورية اليوم بالتزامن مع إنطلاق الثورة السورية |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oCA4iSugabU |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة احتجاج في دمشق تطالب بالحريات |author=BBC |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/arabic/middleeast/2011/03/110315_syria_protest.shtml |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref> Atassi paid tribute to "the Syrian people who took the initiative ahead of the opposition," recalling the popular uprisings that shook [[Tunisian Revolution| Tunisia]] and [[2011 Egyptian revolution| Egypt]]<ref name="ahram" />After the first day of the protests there were reports about approximately 3000 arrests and a few "[[martyrs| martyrs]]", but there are no official figures on the number of deaths.<ref>{{cite news |title=معلومات عن سقوط شهداء في تظاهرات الثلاثاء في سوريا |author=sawtbeirut.com |url=http://sawtbeirut.com/news-in-arabic/world-now/16483-2011-03-16-18-33-05.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 16 March ====
Syrian authorities forcibly dispersed a crowd composed of 200 demonstrators in front of the Syrian Interior Ministry. [[al-Arabiya]] reported that protesters were a mix of activists and jurists, writers, journalists, young academics and family members to people detained in Syrian prisons.<ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian Security Forces Break Up Damascus Protest |author=voanews.com |url=http://www.voanews.com/english/news/middle-east/Syrian-Security-Forces-Break-Up-Damascus-Protest-118085199.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Rare public protest quashed in Syria |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011316131236735771.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian security forces disperse Damascus demo |author=AFP |url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5hN_8F5MkEvOPFuMqr-9HStxVy3iw?docId=CNG.e314ba0dc0a9061349e6fe1072be714a.5f1 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> Several security officers managed to infiltrate themselves in demonstrations at different places and started shouting slogans declaring love and loyalty to President [[Bashar al-Assad| al-Assad]].<ref>{{cite news |title=بلطجية بشار |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s0NcdFYFR0Q |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=بلطجية بشار يفرقونا ويهتفون باسمه |author=Facebook Video |url=http://www.facebook.com/video/video.php?v=110837738996912&oid=420796315726&comments |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> The security forces arrested a number of protestors, [[Al Jazeera]] reported 25<ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة أمام القنصلية السورية في اسطنبول |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CyXg8Hm8Hjs |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>, while [[Al Arabiya]] said 32<ref>{{cite news |title= أسماء الناشطين المعتقلين من قبل قوات الأمن السورية |author=Youtube/Al Arabiya |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OJCttMTen2w |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> including activist and lawyer [[Suhair Atassi]] and Kamal Cheikho, an activist who was released two days earlier.<ref>{{cite news |title=أهالي سجناء وناشطون حقوقيون شاركوا في التجمع |author=Al Arabiya |url=http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/03/16/141801.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=اعتقالات وتفريق مظاهرة في دمشق |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/04E7BD6C-CA0E-4597-A298-0494DD715056.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=سورية: مظاهرة امام وزارة الداخلية في العاصمة دمشق |author=BBC |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/arabic/middleeast/2011/03/110316_syria_prisoners_families.shtml |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> [[World Organisation Against Torture]] published list of arrests and demanded immediate release of them.<ref name="OMCT" />

''Mohamed al-Ali'', a spokesman for the Syrian Interior authority denied that it has happened any demonstrations in Syria and that the Facebook-campaign has been proved unsuccessful. According to the spokesperson, the "claimed protests" consisted of a bunch of few people who were "hiding" among the already packed souq and tried to make it look like a demonstration.<ref>{{cite news |title=تصريح العميد العلي عن مظاهرة وزارة الداخلية |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WevqUv1gGmw |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> In another statement, he went further by saying that the demonstration which was outside the Interior authority was actually in support of President [[Bashar al-Assad]].<ref>{{cite news |title=وزارة الداخلية السورية - المظاهرات كانت لتأييد الرئيس |author=Youtube/BBC |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DXU4ascw1G0
 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 18 March ====
On 18 March the most serious unrest to take place in Syria for decades erupted.<ref name=aljaz18311>{{cite news |title= Clashes erupt at protests in Syria |author=Aljazeera |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/201131817214964640.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=18 March 2011}}</ref> A call for a "Friday of Rage" had been made by anti-goverment protesters, to begin after Friday prayers. In response thousands of protesters in several Syrian cities took to the streets chanting  "God, Syria, Freedom" and anti-corruption slogans.<ref name=bbc18311>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12791738|title=Middle East unrest: Two killed at protest in Syria |publisher=BBC News|date=18 March 2011 |accessdate=18 March 2011}}</ref> In Damascus, security forces broke into the Omayyad Mosque and crashed protesters violently. In the southern city of Daraa, people chanted against Rami Makhlouf, the cousin of the Syrian president. The regime replied by sending Helicopters and water cannons. There were reports that four protesters had been killed and hundreds injured.<ref name=aljaz18311/><ref name=bbc18311/>

==Reaction==
On 29 January 2011, news channel [[Al Arabiya]] reported that Internet connection was cut off, that was quickly denied by official sources.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=72236 |title=Internet is Available across Syria |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref> 
''Al Jazeera'' reported increased security for the planned "Days of rage"<ref>{{cite web |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/features/2011/02/201129103121562395.html |title=Syria: 'A kingdom of silence' |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-02-09}}</ref> . Days before the planned protests, Syrian authorities arrested several political activists, such as businessman Ghassan al-Najar, leader of the Islamic Democratic movement,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.elaph.com/Web/news/2011/2/629499.html |title=Arrest of leader of the Islamic Democratic movement in Syria |publisher=elaph.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37876 |title=Transfer of activist Ghassan Najar to Adra prison after being referred for trial |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> the writer Ali al-Abdallah,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37861 |title=Start of the trial exhibits and the Syrian writer Ali Abdullah |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> Abbas Abbas, from [[Syrian Communist Party]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37788 |title=Jailed prominent Syrian opposition for seven and a half years |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> and several other political personalities of Kurdish backgrounds, such as Adnan Mustafa.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37802 |title=Syrian authorities detain national identity Adnan Mustafa Abu Ammar |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref>

On February 14, blogger and student [[Tal al-Mallohi]] was sentenced to five years in prison for spying for the United States. Washington denied these allegations and asked for the release Tal immediately. February 15, under pressure from human rights organizations, the Syrian government released ''Ghassan al-Najjar'', a "veteran Islamist activist" after he went on hunger strike following his arrest for calling for mass protests.<ref>{{cite news | url = http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/02/16/us-protests-idUSTRE71F41K20110216?pageNumber=2 | title = Egypt-inspired protests gain pace across region | first = Paul | last = Taylor | date = 2011-02-16 | publisher = Reuters }}</ref> February 16, regime critic and director of the Organisation for Democracy and Freedom in Syria (ODFS) [[Ribal al-Assad]], the son of [[Rifaat al-Assad]] and cousin to Syrian President [[Bashar al-Assad]] held a press conference in London, where he made it clear that he "does not want to see a Syrian revolution, but a peaceful change of power".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.elaph.com/Web/news/2011/2/632409.html |title=Opposition leader Ribal Al-Assad is optimistic to changes in Syria |publisher=elaph.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-16}}</ref>

Under pressure from Internet activists that [[Al Jazeera]] has not acknowledged the Syrian protests, special coverage was released on [[Hama massacre]] as well as analysis of the largest [[opposition parties]] in Syria which might have great political influence in any change of power, [[Syrian Communist Party]], [[Muslim Brotherhood]], [[National Salvation Front in Syria| National Salvation Front]], [[Movement for Justice and Development in Syria| Movement for Justice and Development]], [[Reform Party of Syria| Reform Party]], [[Arab Socialist Movement]], [[Arab Socialist Union (Syria)| Arab Socialist Union]], [[Workers Revolutionary Party]], [[Communist party (disambiguation)#Labor.2C Labour| Communist Labour Party]] among others.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gKzsLSDbd8E |title=الثورة السورية.. اتصال بالجزيرة فداكي ياسوريا |publisher=Youtube |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/31339303-299F-413A-8986-49DE94D1F04D.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=مجزرة حماة.. وقائع منسية |publisher=Al Jazeera|date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/605F9657-EC6C-4754-A38B-989C563CB129.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=قوى المعارضة في سوريا |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> On March 9, [[Al Jazeera]] continued its reporting with analysis of opinion detainees in Syria<ref>{{cite news |title=معتقلو الرأي في سوريا |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/0175E8AF-1D2E-4CB2-9473-8EC8528A5224.htm |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=9 March 2011}}</ref> and two days later another special report said that many activists showed their displeasure to the general decree of amnesty did not include political prisoners.<ref>{{cite news |title=ضيق لاستبعاد سياسيين من عفو سوريا |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/C6F6E23B-AC60-4F4F-B3E2-C8F3080DCD2E.htm |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=11 March 2011}}</ref>
