The '''2011 Syrian protests''' are a series of major protests taking place in [[Syria]], which began on 26 January 2011 and are [[Tunisia Effect|influenced]] by [[2010–2011 Middle East and North Africa protests|other concurrent protests in the region]]. The protests have been described as "unprecedented".<ref name=afp19311>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5imPTFixkG47Y44ZniAQVv4wC6UGw?docId=CNG.301dc17b5d60dc6790da1726987ffe7d.c71| title = Syria funeral hit with teargas, protesters wounded: report | publisher =AFP|date=19 March 2011 |accessdate=19 March 2011 }}</ref>

Syria has been under Emergency Law since 1962, effectively suspending most constitutional protections for citizens. [[Hafez al-Assad]] was in power for 30 years and his son [[Bashar al-Assad]] has been in power for the past 10 years.

Protests on March 18 and 19 were the largest to take place in Syria for decades and Syrian authorities responded with violence against protesters. The [[United Nations]] Secretary-General [[Ban Ki-moon]] called the use of deadly force "unacceptable".<ref>[http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011318231622114396.html UN chief slams Syria's crackdown on protests]. Al Jazeera 18 Mar 2011</ref>

==Background==
{{See also|Human rights in Syria}}

Syria has been under Emergency Law since 1962, effectively suspending most constitutional protections for citizens. In 1963, following the [[February 1963 Iraqi coup d'état|Ba'athist overthrow]] success in Iraq, the Ba'ath party [[1963 Syrian coup d'état|took power]] in Syria. Despite internal power changes, like the [[1966 Syrian coup d'état|1966 coup]] and [[1970 Syrian Corrective Revolution]] the Ba'ath party has remained the sole authority in Syria. Since [[1970 Syrian Corrective Revolution|1970 Revolution]], [[Hafez al-Assad]] had led Syria for 30 years, followed by his son [[Bashar al-Assad]] for the past 10 years.

In 1982, after a 6 year period of Islamic insurgency in the country, the [[Hama massacre]] took place as a climax of those events. Hafez al-Assad responded to a [[Muslim Brotherhood]] uprising in the city of [[Hama]], by sending a paramilitary force which killed tens of thousands of people.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.meforum.org/683/syrian-reform-what-lies-beneath |first=Farid N. |last=Ghadry |title=Syrian Reform: What Lies Beneath |date=Winter 2005 |publisher=The Middle East Quarterly }}</ref><ref name="The Sturdy House That Assad Built">{{cite web |url=http://www.foreignaffairs.com/articles/67561/michael-broening/the-sturdy-house-that-assad-built |title=The Sturdy House That Assad Built |date= 7 March 2011 |author=Michael Bröning |publisher=The Foreign Affairs }}</ref>

In 2004, [[2004 Al-Qamishli riots|anti-government riots]] erupted in north-eastern city of [[Al-Qamishli]]. The riot began during a chaotic soccer match, when some people started raising separatist Kurdish flags, hailing [[Massoud Barzani|Barzani]] and [[Talabani]] and US President [[George W. Bush]], turning the match into political conflict. In the aftermath of a brutal reaction by Syrian police and clashes between Kurdish and Arab groups at least 30 people were killed,<ref>{{cite news | first = | last = | author = James Brandon | title = The PKK and Syria's Kurds | url = http://www.jamestown.org/terrorism/news/article.php?articleid=2370250 | publisher = Terrorism Monitor, ''The Jamestown Foundation'' | location = Washington, DC 20036, USA | page = Volume 5, Issue 3 | date = February 15, 2007}}</ref> though other claims indicate on casualty rate of about 100.<ref>MidEastWeb. ''Kurdish agony - the forgotten massacre of Qamishlo''. March 2004.[http://www.mideastweb.org/log/archives/00000231.htm] MidEastWeb</ref>

Bashar al-Assad has tightened Internet censorship with laws such as forcing Internet cafes to record all the comments users post on chat forums.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://en.rsf.org/predator-bashar-al-assad,37213.html |title=Bashar Al-Assad , President, Syria |publisher=Reporters Without Borders }}</ref>
While he has permitted existence of radio stations playing Western pop music, websites such as [[Wikipedia]], [[Youtube]], [[Facebook]] and [[Amazon.com|Amazon]] had been blocked until recently, when, starting on January 1st, all citizens have been able to sign up for high speed internet, and those sites were allowed .<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.economist.com/node/11792330 |title=Red lines that cannot be crossed - The authorities don’t want you to read or see too much |date=24 July 2008 |publisher=The Economist}}</ref>

On 31 January 2011, the ''[[Wall Street Journal]]'' ran an exclusive interview with Bashar al-Assad, [[President]] of [[Syria]], in which he said that it was time to reform. In a comment on the protests in [[Protests in Egypt|Egypt]], [[2010–2011 Tunisian uprising|Tunisia]] and [[2011 Yemeni protests|Yemen]], he said that a "new era" is coming to the [[Middle East]], and that Arab rulers would need to do more to accommodate their people's rising political and economic aspirations.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052748703833204576114712441122894.html |title=Interview With Syrian President Bashar al-Assad |publisher=wsj.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-31}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052748704832704576114340735033236.html |title=Syria Strongman: Time for 'Reform' |publisher=wsj.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-31}}</ref> However, President Bashar al-Assad also declared that his state is immune from the kinds of mass protests that have taken place in Egypt.<ref>[http://www.foxnews.com/opinion/2011/02/02/middle-east-protests-syria-egypt/ FOX News opinion].</ref>

== Protests ==
=== Timeline ===
==== 26–28 January ====
On 26 January 2011, Hasan Ali Akleh from [[Al-Hasakah]] poured gasoline and burned himself in the same way [[Mohamed Bouazizi]] had in Tunis. According to eyewitnesses, the action was "a protest against the [[Syrian government]]".<ref name="Hasan Ali Akleh 1">{{cite web|url=http://www.free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37797 |title=Information on the death of a young man who burned himself in Al Hasakah |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref><ref name="Hasan Ali Akleh 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.metransparent.com/spip.php?page=article&id_article=12661&lang=ar |title=Syrian suicider is "Hasan Ali Akleh". Damascus has banned a demonstration in support of Egypt |publisher=metransparent.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref>

On 28 January 2011, a demonstration was held in [[Ar-Raqqah]] during the evening, in protest against the killing of two soldiers of [[Kurdish people|Kurdish]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37803 |title=Demonstration in Ar-Raqqa, Syria |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref>

==== 2 February ====
On 2 February, [[Human Rights Watch]] reported a group of 20 people in civilian clothing had beaten and dispersed 15 people who had been holding a candlelight vigil at [[Bab Tuma]] in Damascus for [[2011 Egyptian revolution| Egyptian demonstrators]].<ref name=guardian240211> {{cite news| url=http://www.guardian.co.uk/world/2011/feb/24/syria-crackdown-protest-arrests-beatings| title=Syria clamps down on dissent with beatings and arrests| publisher=The Guardian| date=24 February 2011| accessdate=24 February 2011 }} </ref>

==== 4–16 February ====
On 3 February a "Day of rage" was called for in Syria from 4–5 February on [[social media]] sites Facebook and Twitter. Protesters demand reform in the government, however most protests so far have remained outside of Syria and have been small.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.msnbc.msn.com/id/41400687/ns/world_news-mideastn_africa/ |title='Day of rage' protest urged in Syria - World news - Mideast/N. Africa - msnbc.com |publisher=MSNBC |date= |accessdate=2011-02-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aysor.am/en/news/2011/02/03/syrian-facebook-twitter/ |title=“Day of Rage” planned for Syria; protests scheduled for Feb 4-5 - aysor.am - Hot news from Armenia |publisher=aysor.am |date= |accessdate=2011-02-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://freedomist.com/2011/01/30/syran-anger-day-planned-feb-5-2011/|title=Syrian Anger Day Planned Feb 5, 2011|author=William R Collier jr|date=January 30, 2011|publisher=The Freedomist|accessdate=January 31, 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://topsy.com/freedomist.com/2011/01/30/syran-anger-day-planned-feb-5-2011/ |title=Syrian Anger Day Planned Feb 5, 2011 | The Freedomist |publisher=TOPSY |date= |accessdate=2011-02-01}}</ref> The protests were expected to begin on 4 February 2011 as [[social media]] mobilised the people of [[Syria]] for rallies demanding freedom, [[human rights]] and the end to the [[state of emergency]]. Protests were also scheduled for 5 February in front of the parliament in [[Damascus]] and at Syrian embassies internationally.<ref>[http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/02/201122171649677912.html "Calls for weekend protests in Syria"].</ref> ''Al Jazeera'' reported increased security for the planned "Days of rage."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/features/2011/02/201129103121562395.html |title=Syria: 'A kingdom of silence' |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-02-09}}</ref>

The only known action took place February 5, where hundreds of protesters in [[Al-Hasakah]] participated in a mass demonstration, calling for the al-Assad's departure. Syrian authorities have arrested dozens and the demonstration was triggered quickly.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37849 |title=Demonstration on the day of anger in Hasaka and Syrian authorities arrested dozens |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-15}}</ref> Suhair Atassi, who runs the banned [[Jamal al-Atassi| Jamal Atassi Forum]], called for political reforms and the reinstatement of civil rights, as well as an end to the emergency law in place since 1963.<ref>{{cite magazine|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/2011/02/201129135657367367.html |title=Q&A: Syrian activist Suhair Atassi |publisher=Al Jazeera English |date=2011-02-09 |accessdate=2011-02-13}}</ref> However, no protests occurred on either date.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.upi.com/Top_News/Special/2011/02/04/Syria-quiet-on-day-of-rage/UPI-58781296853501/ |title=Syria quiet on day of rage |publisher=UPI.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.irishtimes.com/newspaper/world/2011/0205/1224289070429.html |title=Call for Syrian 'day of rage' as planned protests fail to ignite - The Irish Times - Sat, Feb 05, 2011 |publisher=The Irish Times |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Karam |first=Zeina |url=http://www.miamiherald.com/2011/02/05/2052033/syria-escapes-day-of-rage-protests.html |title=Syria escapes 'Day of Rage' protests - World Wires |publisher=MiamiHerald.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref> After the failure of attempts to a "day of rage," ''[[Al Jazeera]]'' called the country "a kingdom of silence" and identified key factors for stability within Syria as being strict security measures, the popularity of President Bashar al-Assad, and fears of potential sectarian violence in the aftermath of a government ouster (akin to neighbouring [[Iraqi insurgency|Iraq]]).<ref>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/features/2011/02/201129103121562395.html |title=Syria: 'A kingdom of silence' |publisher=aljazeera.net |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref>

==== 17 February ====
{{External media
| video1 = {{YouTube|8Cwm5Ck2sSs|Protests in Damscus, Syria, February 17, 2011}}
}}
On February 17, a spontaneous demonstration took place outside [[Al-Hamidiyah Souq]] in Damascus, in protest at the police beating of a local shop owner. Several men gathered around and blocked the road, while they chanted "The [[Syrian people]] will not be humiliated". An eyewitness estimated the number to more than 1,500 demonstrators. [[Secret police]] officers arrived quickly to the scene along with several government officials and finally Syria's interior minister who dispersed the demonstrators and promised an investigation.<ref name=guardian240211/><ref name="ROBERT MACKEY"> {{cite news
| last=  MACKEY
| first= ROBERT
| url=  http://thelede.blogs.nytimes.com/2011/02/17/latest-updates-on-middle-east-protests-4/?partner=rss&emc=rss
| title=  Feb. 17 Updates on Middle East Protest
| publisher=  [[nytimes.com]]
| date=  February 17, 2011
| accessdate= }} </ref> The minister took the shop owner in his car and promised to investigate what had happened.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37926 |title=After the attack on a young man, beating and humiliation: the hundreds of Syrians demonstrating in Harika |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-17}}</ref> A couple of hours later, several video versions were posted on [[YouTube]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qDHLsU-ik_Y |title=Demonstration in Damscus Syria |publisher=[[YouTube]]|date= |accessdate=2011-02-17}}</ref>

==== 22–23 February ====
On February 22 , around 200 people gathered outside the Libyan embassy in [[Damascus]] to protest against the Libyan regime and wanted the ambassador to resign. Despite the peaceful message, there were nearly twice as many secret and uniformed police as protesters. The demonstrators carried placards reading "Freedom for the people" and "Down with [[Gaddafi]]". Everything took a new turn when the protesters started chanting "Traitors are those that beat their people.", which created nervousness among the security forces who immediately asked people to leave to their homes. 14 people were arrested and several more beaten by policemen. Some protesters were punched, kicked and beaten with sticks. All present had their identities recorded. Fourteen people were detained but later released.<ref name=guardian240211/>
<ref> {{cite web
| url=  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UNLUCg-TrZU&feature=player_embedded#at=20
| title= الاعتصام امام السفارة الليبية دمشق-22-شباط  Part 5
| publisher=  Youtube
| date=   2011/02/24
| accessdate =
}} </ref><ref> {{cite web
| url=  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLkLqI07erk
| title= الاعتصام امام السفارة الليبية دمشق-22-شباط  Part 8
| publisher=  Youtube
| date=   2011/02/24
| accessdate =
}} </ref>

On February 23, during a routine session of the [[Parliament of Syria| Syrian parliament]], a member proposed that harsh [[State of emergency| emergency laws]] to be reviewed. The issue was not scheduled for discussion, and it came as a surprise. A pro-government official at the session recalled it with anger. The proposal was rapidly quashed, when the speaker put the proposed review to a vote in the chamber, none of the other 249 MPs supported it.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thenational.ae/news/worldwide/middle-east/syrian-mp-calls-for-review-of-harsh-emergency-laws |title=Syrian MP calls for review of harsh emergency laws |publisher=The National |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>

==== 6–10 March ====
On March 6, a number of young boys, under 15, were arrested in [[Daraa]] because they have written infamous slogan "the people want to overthrow the regime" on the walls of the city.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/394E1B63-488A-4FF1-BD39-0CD306261FFE.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=سوريا تعتقل شبانا في درعا |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> Libyan [[Anti-Gaddafi forces]] shot down two Syrian war planes in [[Ras Lanuf]], that was later denied by Syrian officials.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.economicvoice.com/libyan-rebels-shoot-down-two-syrian-war-planes/50017136#axzz1FwTDTTfU |title=Libyan rebels shoot down two Syrian war planes |publisher=Economic Voice |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://syria-news.com/readnews.php?sy_seq=129725 |title=مصدر رسمي سوري ينفي مشاركة طيارين سوريين بالقتال في ليبيا |publisher=Syria-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.commentarymagazine.com/2011/03/06/syrian-pilots-shot-down-in-libya/
 |title=Syrian pilots shot down in Libya? |publisher=Commentary Magazine |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> Four brothers that are Syrian opposition activists went missing shortly after passing out flyers in Beirut in front of the Syrian embassy, calling for a demonstration to oppose Syria's government.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.monstersandcritics.com/news/middleeast/news/article_1624021.php/Search-on-for-Syrian-opposition-activists-missing-in-Lebanon |title=Search on for Syrian opposition activists missing in Lebanon |publisher=Middle East News |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref>[[Time (magazine)| Time]] says that the commitment can still be found among the Syrian youth, but what is needed is a starting point.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.time.com/time/world/article/0,8599,2057454,00.html |title=The Youth of Syria: The Rebels Are on Pause |publisher=Time.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> [[Ribal al-Assad]] says that it is almost time for Syria to be next [[Domino theory| domino]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/opinion/2011/03/20113482455647372.html |title=Is Syria the next domino? |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref>

On March 7, 13 Syrian political prisoners have gone on [[hunger strike]] to protest against "political detentions and oppression" in Syria. They demanded an end to political arrests, the removal of injustices and the restitution of rights that have been removed from civil and political life<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.jpost.com/Headlines/Article.aspx?id=211153 |title=Syrian prisoners launch hunger strike |publisher=Jerusalem Post |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.marxist.com/syria-prisoners-of-conscience-declare-hunger-strike.htm |title=Syria: Prisoners of Conscience in Damascus Central Prison declare hunger strike |publisher=marxist.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref>

On March 10, dozens of jailed Kurds in Syria, from the ''Yakiti party'' and the ''Democratic Union'', started a hunger strike in solidarity with other activists who have mounted the same action in a prison near Damascus three days earlier.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5j_unWcGEmBcNfMzgi2zu5xlRNDzw?docId=CNG.b10f77bcf3bb312a68aa8c85b50004d3.8e1 |title=Jailed Kurds on Syria hunger strike: rights group |publisher=AFP |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Human Rights Watch]] reacts to the disappearance of Syrian activists in Lebanon four days earlier and fears that [[Lebanon]] is "back to doing Syria's dirty job".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hrw.org/en/news/2011/03/09/lebanon-reveal-fate-disappeared-syrians |title=Lebanon: Reveal Fate of Disappeared Syrians |publisher=HRW |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> Syrian Foreign Ministry stated that Syria is monitoring with high concern "the [[2011 Libyan uprising| tragic developments]] in the brotherly country of [[Libya]]".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=77422 |title=Syria respects Libya's Sovereignty |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> Syrian newspaper [[Al-Watan (Syria)| Al-Watan]] said that  the Syrian government have welcomed the fall of [[Mubarak]]'s regime and are might be looking forward new leadership that does not "cover for Israeli violations".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://http://www.syria-today.com/index.php/politics/14482-life-after-mubarak |title=Life After Mubarak |publisher=syria-today.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Iran| Iranian]] official said that they support Syria's right to "restore the occupied [[Golan Heights| Golan]] under its sovereignty".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=77335 |title=Iran support Syria to restore the occupied Golan |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Reform Party of Syria]] said that "al-Assad is sending arms to [[Gaddafi]] to kill his people with". <ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.worldtribune.com/worldtribune/WTARC/2011/me_libya0257_03_10.asp |title=Syrian pilots said to be flying Libyan fighter jets |publisher=World Tribune |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>

==== 12–13 March ====
On March 12, thousands of Syrian Kurds in [[al-Qamishli]] and [[al-Hasakah]] protested on the day of Kurdish martyr, an annual event since 2004, when many Syrian kurds died in anti-government demonstrations.<ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة في الجزيرة السورية 12 اذار 2011 |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7TXnYz5OsI8 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=حفلة تأبين شهداء إنتفاضة قامشلو  |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MWKh8C5Z6ps |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=الكورد السوريون يحييون ذكرى انتفاضتهم السابعة بايقاد الشموع اجلالاً و اكراماُ لارواح الشهداء |author=soparo.com |url=http://ar.soparo.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=17803%3A2011-03-11-23-36-17&catid=34%3A2008-05-23-23-58-43&Itemid=192 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> [[Al Jazeera]] reported that a civilian vessel, loaded with weapons and ammunition and 500 [[SUV]]s had sailed from the port of [[Tartus]] in Syria towards [[Tripoli]] in Libya, in order to supply [[Military of Libya| Gaddafi forces]]. The reporter said that the Syrian battalion fighting alongside [[Gaddafi]] against the [[Anti-Gaddafi forces| Libyan rebels]].<ref>{{cite news |title=النظام الاسدي الفاسد يقتل الشعب الليبي باسلحته |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j0ZfsdCXFJA |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=دعم النظام السوري لسفاح ليبيا |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qE6C7LNCRFQ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> That was also confirmed with an interview with Libyan politician Ibrahim Jibreel.<ref>{{cite news |title=الجزيرة ـ إبراهيم جبريل والتواطؤ السوري مع نظام القذافي |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZaPyJhLIVdA&feature=player_embedded#at=387
 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref>

On March 13, Kamal Hussein Cheikho, a Kurdish member of the Committee for the Defence of Democratic Liberties and Human Rights in Syria (CDF), was released on bail of 500 Syrian pounds ($10). He is still facing charges for allegedly publishing material harmful to the country.<ref name="OMCT">{{cite news |title=Syrian Arab Republic: Assault and arrest of dozens of family members of human rights defenders and prisoners of conscience |author=OMCT |url=http://www.omct.org/human-rights-defenders/urgent-interventions/syrian-arab-republic/2011/03/d21153/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syria blogger arrest condemned |author=http://mybroadband.co.za |url=http://mybroadband.co.za/news/internet/18663-Syria-blogger-arrest-condemned.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=24 February 2011}}</ref><ref name="ahram">{{cite news |title=Arrests as Syria sees second day of rare protests |author=ahram.org.eg |url=http://english.ahram.org.eg/NewsContent/2/8/7879/World/Region/Arrests-as-Syria-sees-second-day-of-rare-protests.aspx |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 15 March ====
<!-- Deleted image removed: [[File:syria protest.jpg|thumb|A group of supporters of Bashar al-Assad, right, and some opposed to his regime, left, outside the Syrian embassy in Cairo, Egypt]]  -->Simultaneous demonstrations took place in major cities across Syria. Thousands of protestors gathered in [[Aleppo]], [[al-Hasakah]], [[Daraa]], [[Deir ez-Zor]], and [[Hama]]. There were some clashes with security, according to reports from dissident groups. In [[Damascus]], a smaller group of 200 men grew spontaneously to about 1,500 men. Damascus has not seen such protests since the 1980s. The official Facebook page called "Syrian Revolution 2011" shown pictures of supportive demonstrations in [[Cairo]], [[Nicosia]], [[Helsinki]], [[Istanbul]] and [[Berlin]]. There were also unconfirmed news that supporters of Libyan descent stormed into the Syrian Embassy in [[Paris]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Fresh Protests Erupt in Syria |author=theepochtimes.com |url=http://www.theepochtimes.com/n2/content/view/53074/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=السوريون يكسرون جدار الخوف بالتظاهر في قلب دمشق |author=Youtube/Al Arabiya |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rRHRxUxRbxo |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=ردّدوا هتافات تدعو لمحاربة الفساد وفتح باب الحريات |author=Al Arabiya |url=http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/03/15/141661.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=الاف السوريين يثورون في قلب دمشق و المحافظات مطالبين بالحرية |author=sawtbeirut.com |url=http://www.sawtbeirut.com/news-in-arabic/world-now/16474-syria-revolution-2011-march-15.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة شباب الثورة في تركيا |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7AbjW0Kuhl8 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

Another recently released political [[Suhair Atassi]] became an unofficial spokesperson for the "Syrian revolution", when she was interviewed by dozens of Arab and international media channels regarding the protests.  There were reports of 6 arrested in Damascus.<ref>{{cite news |title=آخر اخبار انتفاضة سورية من فضائية الجزيرة |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sMNBN1PhjzY |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=تقرير قناه ال BBC للانتفاضه السوريه |author=Youtube/BBC |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iAUOqfRSEaM |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة أمام السفارة السورية اليوم بالتزامن مع إنطلاق الثورة السورية |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oCA4iSugabU |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة احتجاج في دمشق تطالب بالحريات |author=BBC |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/arabic/middleeast/2011/03/110315_syria_protest.shtml |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref> Atassi paid tribute to "the Syrian people who took the initiative ahead of the opposition," recalling the popular uprisings that shook [[Tunisian Revolution| Tunisia]] and [[2011 Egyptian revolution| Egypt]]<ref name="ahram" />After the first day of the protests there were reports about approximately 3000 arrests and a few "[[martyrs| martyrs]]", but there are no official figures on the number of deaths.<ref>{{cite news |title=معلومات عن سقوط شهداء في تظاهرات الثلاثاء في سوريا |author=sawtbeirut.com |url=http://sawtbeirut.com/news-in-arabic/world-now/16483-2011-03-16-18-33-05.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 16 March ====
Syrian authorities forcibly dispersed a crowd composed of 200 demonstrators in front of the Syrian Interior Ministry. [[al-Arabiya]] reported that protesters were a mix of activists and jurists, writers, journalists, young academics and family members to people detained in Syrian prisons.<ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian Security Forces Break Up Damascus Protest |author=voanews.com |url=http://www.voanews.com/english/news/middle-east/Syrian-Security-Forces-Break-Up-Damascus-Protest-118085199.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Rare public protest quashed in Syria |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011316131236735771.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian security forces disperse Damascus demo |author=AFP |url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5hN_8F5MkEvOPFuMqr-9HStxVy3iw?docId=CNG.e314ba0dc0a9061349e6fe1072be714a.5f1 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> Several security officers managed to infiltrate themselves in demonstrations at different places and started shouting slogans declaring love and loyalty to President Bashar al-Assad.<ref>{{cite news |title=بلطجية بشار |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s0NcdFYFR0Q |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=بلطجية بشار يفرقونا ويهتفون باسمه |author=Facebook Video |url=http://www.facebook.com/video/video.php?v=110837738996912&oid=420796315726&comments |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> The security forces arrested a number of protestors, [[Al Jazeera]] reported 25<ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة أمام القنصلية السورية في اسطنبول |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CyXg8Hm8Hjs |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>, while [[Al Arabiya]] said 32<ref>{{cite news |title= أسماء الناشطين المعتقلين من قبل قوات الأمن السورية |author=Youtube/Al Arabiya |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OJCttMTen2w |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> including activist and lawyer [[Suhair Atassi]] and Kamal Cheikho, an activist who was released two days earlier.<ref>{{cite news |title=أهالي سجناء وناشطون حقوقيون شاركوا في التجمع |author=Al Arabiya |url=http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/03/16/141801.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=اعتقالات وتفريق مظاهرة في دمشق |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/04E7BD6C-CA0E-4597-A298-0494DD715056.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=سورية: مظاهرة امام وزارة الداخلية في العاصمة دمشق |author=BBC |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/arabic/middleeast/2011/03/110316_syria_prisoners_families.shtml |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> [[World Organisation Against Torture]] published list of arrests and demanded immediate release of them.<ref name="OMCT" />

''Mohamed al-Ali'', a spokesman for the Syrian Interior authority denied that it has happened any demonstrations in Syria and that the Facebook-campaign has been proved unsuccessful. According to the spokesperson, the "claimed protests" consisted of a bunch of few people who were "hiding" among the already packed souq and tried to make it look like a demonstration.<ref>{{cite news |title=تصريح العميد العلي عن مظاهرة وزارة الداخلية |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WevqUv1gGmw |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> In another statement, he went further by saying that the demonstration which was outside the Interior authority was actually in support of President Bashar al-Assad.<ref>{{cite news |title=وزارة الداخلية السورية - المظاهرات كانت لتأييد الرئيس |author=Youtube/BBC |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DXU4ascw1G0
 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 18 March ====
On March 18, the most serious unrest to take place in Syria for decades erupted.<ref name=aljaz18311>{{cite news |title= Clashes erupt at protests in Syria |author=Aljazeera |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/201131817214964640.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=18 March 2011}}</ref> After online calls for a "Friday of dignity", thousands of protesters demanding an end to alleged government corruption took to the streets of cities across Syria after Friday prayers.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oC55uPBKYqU |title=Violence flares at Syrian protest |publisher=Youtube/Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref> The protesters were met with a violent crackdown orchestrated by state security forces. The protesters chanted  "God, Syria, Freedom" and anti-corruption slogans.<ref name=bbc18311>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12791738|title=Middle East unrest: Two killed at protest in Syria |publisher=BBC News|date=18 March 2011 |accessdate=18 March 2011}}</ref>

Amateur video footage posted on [[YouTube]] and [[Twitter]] showed large groups of protesters in several cities, such as [[Damascus]], [[Daraa]], [[Homs]], [[Baniyas]], [[Aleppo]], [[al-Qamishli]] and [[Deir ez-Zor]].<ref name= "telegraph110318">{{cite web|url= http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/middleeast/syria/8391438/Syria-four-dead-in-rare-demonstrations.html |title=Syria: 'four dead' in rare demonstrations |publisher=The Telegraph |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref><ref name="ap110318">{{cite web|url= http://www.google.com/hostednews/ap/article/ALeqM5iFVmeKhYv0amn9vzUTAcH4HeEz4Q?docId=3b597c45357b466eae7fcd1d6f4d433d |title= 3 protesters killed in Syria, activist says |publisher=Associated Press |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= http://www.ibtimes.com/articles/124507/20110318/syria.htm |title = Anti-government protests slowly erupting in Syria |publisher= IB Times |date= |accessdate=2011-3-18}}</ref> In [[Damascus]], plain-clothes Syrian security forces broke up protests outside the [[Umayyad Mosque]], dragging away at least two activists.<ref name="telegraph110318">{{cite web|url=http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/middleeast/syria/8391438/Syria-four-dead-in-rare-demonstrations.html | title = Syria: 'four dead' in rare demonstrations | publisher = The Telegraph |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref> In the southern city of [[Daraa]], people chanted against [[Rami Makhlouf]], the cousin of al-Assad. The regime responded with helicopters and water cannons. There were unconfirmed reports that four protesters had been killed and hundreds injured.<ref name =aljaz18311/><ref name=bbc18311 /> A resident said that Syrian security forces in killed three protesters in Daraa.<ref name=aljaz18311/> The Syrian Government claimed that unnamed "infiltrators" had been attempting to cause chaos.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://af.reuters.com/article/energyOilNews/idAFLDE72H1U520110318?sp=true | title = Syrian forces kill three protesters in southern city |publisher=Reuters |date= |accessdate=2011-3-18}}</ref>

==== 19 March ====
On 19 March Syrian security forces fired tear gas to disperse crowds in [[Daraa]] following the funeral of two anti-government protesters killed by security forces on the previous day.<ref name=bbc19311>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12796020| title = Syria unrest: Tear gas fired at Deraa funeral | publisher =BBC News|date=19 March 2011 |accessdate=19 March 2011 }}</ref> The crowds had been shouting "God, Syria, freedom" before the security forces intervened.<ref name=bbc19311/> Witnesses said that the gas used appeared to be more toxic than ordinary tear gas.<ref name=nyt19311>{{cite web|url=http://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/20/world/middleeast/20syria.html?_r=1&hp| title = Syrian Police Attack Mourners| publisher =The New York Times|date=19 March 2011 |accessdate=19 March 2011 }}</ref> The Syrian League for Human Rights reported that 10 women who had been detained on 16 March following a rally outside the interior ministry had begun a hunger strike.<ref name=afp19311/>

==== 20 March ====
On 20 March thousands took to the streets in the city of Daraa for a third day, shouting slogans against the country's emergency law. One person was killed and scores injured as security forces opened fire on protesters.<ref name="AJSyria20March">{{cite news |title=Syria to free child prisoners |author=[[Al-Jazeera English]] |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011320113138901721.html |newspaper= |date=20 March 2011 |accessdate=20 March 2011}}</ref> The courthouse, the Baath party headquarters in the city, and Rami Makhlouf's [[Syriatel]] building were all set on fire.<ref>http://www.theglobeandmail.com/news/world/africa-mideast/syrian-protesters-set-fire-to-ruling-partys-headquarters-palace-of-justice/article1948865/</ref>

==== 21 March ====
Protests started to spread further across the country.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://af.reuters.com/article/worldNews/idAFTRE72K6MP20110321?pageNumber=1&virtualBrandChannel=0| title = Allies desert Yemeni leader, Syria protests spread| publisher =Reuters|date=21 March 2011 |accessdate=21 March 2011 }}</ref> Thousands of people took to the streets in Daraa and troops were sent to the city.<ref name=irtimes22311>{{cite web|url=http://www.irishtimes.com/newspaper/world/2011/0322/1224292773018.html| title = US condemns Syria crackdown| publisher =Irish Times|date=22 March 2011 |accessdate=22 March 2011 }}</ref> Hundreds of people protested in Jassem and there were reports of protests in Aleppo, Der Al-Zour, Banias, Homs and Hama.<ref name=irtimes22311/><ref>{{cite news|title=Protests spread to southern Syrian town Jassem|newspaper=Ya Libnan|date=21 March 2011|url=http://www.yalibnan.com/2011/03/21/protests-spread-to-southern-syrian-town-jassem/|accessdate=21 March 2011}}</ref> 

It was reported that an 11-year-old boy had died of wounds suffered when Syrian security forces dispersed a protest rally in Daraa,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5ipLl3yeb_p-BMEyqG7WM2O9PYSbg?docId=CNG.e623321b6a9f7efb561fd470f40817bf.9d1| title = US condemns Syria crackdown| publisher =AFP|date=21 March 2011 |accessdate=21 March 2011 }}</ref> and meanwhile, the Beirut-based [[al Akhbar]] newspaper said that caretaker PM [[Saad Hariri]]'s Future Movement paid the protesters,<ref>http://www1.albawaba.com/main-headlines/syrian-army-deploys-around-daraa-lebanon-pm-accused-financing-riots-syria</ref> something the PM said was rubbish.

==== 22 March ====
There were protests gathered in Daraa, [[Nawa, Syria|Nawa]], Sanamain and Jassem.<ref name=aljaz22311>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011322155925612161.html| title =UN calls for Syria probe as hundreds protest  | publisher =Al Jazeera|date=22 March 2011 |accessdate=22 March 2011 }}</ref> 
<ref name=cnn22311>{{cite web|url=http://edition.cnn.com/2011/WORLD/meast/03/22/syria.protests/?hpt=T2| title = Syria arrests opposition leader as protests continue| publisher =CNN|date=22 March 2011 |accessdate=22 March 2011 }}</ref> Navi Pillay, the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights, called for an investigation into the deaths of six protesters who had been killed by Syrian security forces in March.<ref name=aljaz22311/> An AFP photographer and cameraman were beaten by security forces in Daraa and had their equipment seized.<ref name=aljaz22311/>

==Reactions==
===Domestic===
On 8 March, [[Syrian Arab News Agency|SANA]], the official Syrian news agency, published an article on its website titled "President [[Bashar al-Assad| al-Assad]] issued a decree provides for a legislative grant amnesty for political crimes committed before the date of 2011-03-08". Three hours later, the publication was removed.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://http://all4syria.info/content/view/40436/96/ |title=سيادة الرئيس يصدر عفواً عن الجرائم السياسية ثم يسحب بعد ثلاث ساعات ؟ |publisher=All4Syria |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}{{ar}}</ref>. Hours later, Syrian authorities released [[Haitham al-Maleh]], an 80-year-old former judge, one of al-Assad's most outspoken critics, under an amnesty marking the anniversary of the [[1963 Syrian coup d'état|1963 coup]] which brought the [[Baath Party]] to power.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12679902 |title=Syrian activist [[Haitham al-Maleh]] freed under amnesty |publisher=BBC News |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/08/us-syria-rights-idUSTRE7274LC20110308 |title=Syria frees 80-year-old former judge in amnesty |publisher=Reuters |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref> Twelve Syrian [[human rights]] organisations called on the government to scrap the [[state of emergency]] which has stifled civil society for almost 50 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5jVJSMH9FyXYejUJ-MoOUniMK6CBg?docId=CNG.08dc880232fd4c049d2f1a10f4641409.cf1 |title=12 rights groups in Syria demand end to emergency |publisher=AFP |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref>

On 12 March, newly released regime critic [[Haitham al-Maleh]] announced in a YouTube video his support and assistance to the Syrian youth who are behind the new wave of protests and hoped that he will soon see democracy in Syria.<ref>{{cite news |title=هيثم المالح .. يوجه رسالة إلى شباب الثورة السورية |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EVYVtaVKnf0 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref>

On 19 March, in response to the growing demonstrations Assad issued a decree shortening the length of mandatory army [[conscription]] from 21 to 18 months.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/19/us-syria-idUSTRE72I22020110319?pageNumber=2| title = Syrian mourners call for revolt, forces fire tear gas| publisher =Reuters|date=19 March 2011 |accessdate=19 March 2011 }}</ref>

On 20 March, the Syrian government declared that it will release 15 children who had been arrested earlier for writing pro-democracy graffiti.<ref name="AJSyria20March"/>

On 22 March, President [[Bashar al-Assad]] fired the Governor [[Faisal Kalthoum]].<ref>http://www1.albawaba.com/main-headlines/unrest-continues-syria</ref> Syrian authorities arrested Loay Hussein, a prominent human rights campaigner.<ref name=cnn22311/>

;Censorship and arrests
On 29 January 2011, ''[[Al Arabiya]]'' reported that Internet connections had been cut off; however, the reported was quickly denied by official sources.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=72236 |title=Internet is Available across Syria |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref>

On 5 February, Internet services were said to have been curbed, although ''[[Facebook]]'' and ''[[YouTube]]'' were reported to have been restored on 8 February.<ref name="JENNIFER PRESTON"> {{cite news
| last=  PRESTON
| first=  JENNIFER
| url=  http://www.nytimes.com/2011/02/10/world/middleeast/10syria.html?_r=1
| title=  Syria Restores Access to Facebook and YouTube
| publisher=  [[The New York Times]]
| date=  9 February 2011
| accessdate= }} </ref> Suggestions were made that easing the ban could be a way to track activists.<ref>{{cite web|author=Jillian York |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/opinion/2011/02/2011212122746819907.html |title=Unblocking Syria's social media |publisher=Al Jazeera English |date= |accessdate=2011-02-13}}</ref>

Days before protests planned for 5 February, Syrian authorities arrested several political activists, such as businessman Ghassan al-Najar, leader of the Islamic Democratic movement,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.elaph.com/Web/news/2011/2/629499.html |title=Arrest of leader of the Islamic Democratic movement in Syria |publisher=elaph.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37876 |title=Transfer of activist Ghassan Najar to Adra prison after being referred for trial |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> the writer Ali al-Abdallah,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37861 |title=Start of the trial exhibits and the Syrian writer Ali Abdullah |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> Abbas Abbas, from the [[Syrian Communist Party]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37788 |title=Jailed prominent Syrian opposition for seven and a half years |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> and several other political personalities of Kurdish backgrounds, such as Adnan Mustafa.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37802 |title=Syrian authorities detain national identity Adnan Mustafa Abu Ammar |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref>

On February 14, blogger and student [[Tal al-Mallohi]] was convicted of spying for the United States and sentenced to five years in prison. Washington denied these allegations and asked for al-Mallohi's immediate release. February 15, under pressure from human rights organizations, the Syrian government released ''Ghassan al-Najjar'', a "veteran Islamist activist" after he went on hunger strike following his arrest for calling for mass protests.<ref>{{cite news | url = http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/02/16/us-protests-idUSTRE71F41K20110216?pageNumber=2 | title = Egypt-inspired protests gain pace across region | first = Paul | last = Taylor | date = 2011-02-16 | publisher = Reuters }}</ref> On February 16, regime critic and director of the Organisation for Democracy and Freedom in Syria (ODFS) [[Ribal al-Assad]], son of [[Rifaat al-Assad]] and cousin to Syrian President Bashar al-Assad, held a press conference in London, in which he made it clear that he "does not want to see a Syrian revolution, but a peaceful change of power".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.elaph.com/Web/news/2011/2/632409.html |title=Opposition leader Ribal Al-Assad is optimistic to changes in Syria |publisher=elaph.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-16}}</ref>

===International===
;Supranational
*{{flagicon|European Union}} [[European Union]] - On 22 March [[Catherine Ashton]], the [[High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy]], issued a statement which said that the European Union "strongly condemns the violent repression, including through the use of live ammunition, of peaceful protests in various locations across Syria".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.middle-east-online.com/english/?id=45066|title=EU condemns 'unacceptable' repression in Syria |publisher=Middle East Online |date=22 March 2011|accessdate=22 March 2011}}</ref>
*{{flagicon|United Nations}} [[United Nations]] – Secretary General chief [[Ban Ki-moon]] called the use of deadly force "unacceptable".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011318231622114396.html |title=UN chief slams Syria's crackdown on protests |publisher=Al Jazeera |date=18 March 2011}}</ref>

;States
*{{flag|Canada}} - On 21 March the Canadian Foreign Affairs Minister Lawrence Cannon stated that "Canada deplores the multiple deaths and injuries following protests in several Syrian cities over the weekend".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5jj7uSbIAaro1v0fwW4jziIxQ1j9w?docId=CNG.a807bd69f3debaa7a6b4ca2383f9500b.1191|title=Canada condemns violence in Yemen, Bahrain, Syria|publisher=AFP |date=21 March 2011|accessdate=22 March 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|France}} - The Foreign Ministry condemned the violence carried out against demonstrators and called for political prisoners to be freed.<ref>{{cite news |title=France condemns violence against protestors in Syria |author=Lebanon News |url=http://www.nowlebanon.com/NewsArchiveDetails.aspx?ID=251195 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=17 March 2011}}</ref>
* {{flag|United States}} - President [[Barack Obama]]'s administration condemned the use of violence, stating “The United States stands for a set of universal rights, including the freedom of expression and assembly, and believes that governments, including the Syrian government, must address the legitimate aspirations of their people".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2011-03-18/obama-administration-condemns-syrian-violence-against-protests.html |title=Obama Administration Condemns Syrian Violence Against Protests |publisher=Bloomberg |date=18 March 2011}}</ref>

;NGOs
*[[Amnesty International]] condemned the "violent crackdown against a peaceful protest" by people calling for the release of political prisoners.<ref>{{cite news |title=Amnesty condemns Syrian crackdown on protest |author=Reuters/Yahoo |url=http://uk.news.yahoo.com/22/20110317/tpl-uk-syria-protest-amnesty-81f3b62.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=17 March 2011}}</ref>
*[[Human Rights Watch]] stated that the Syrian government has shown "no qualms about shooting dead its own citizens for speaking out." It also said that Syrians have shown "incredible courage in daring to protest publicly against one of the most repressive governments in the region, and they shouldn't have to pay with their lives."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rferl.org/content/rights_groups_condemn_syria/2345012.html |title=Rights Groups Condemn Syria's Violent Crackdown On Protesters |publisher=RFE/RL |date=21 March 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.hrw.org/en/news/2011/03/20/syria-government-crackdown-leads-protester-deaths |title=Syria: Government Crackdown Leads to Protester Deaths |publisher=Human Rights Watch |date=20 March 2011 }}</ref>

;Media
Under criticism from Internet activists for failing to acknowledge the Syrian protests, ''[[Al Jazeera]]'' has broadcast special coverage  of the Hama massacre as well as analysis of the largest [[opposition parties]] in Syria which might have great political influence in any change of power: the [[Syrian Communist Party]], [[Muslim Brotherhood#Syria|Muslim Brotherhood]], [[National Salvation Front in Syria| National Salvation Front]], [[Movement for Justice and Development in Syria|Movement for Justice and Development]], [[Reform Party of Syria|Reform Party]], [[Arab Socialist Movement]], [[Arab Socialist Union (Syria)|Arab Socialist Union]], [[Workers Revolutionary Party]], [[Communist party (disambiguation)#Labor.2C Labour|Communist Labour Party]], and others.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/605F9657-EC6C-4754-A38B-989C563CB129.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=قوى المعارضة في سوريا |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> On March 9, [[Al Jazeera]] continued its reporting with analysis of opinion detainees in Syria<ref>{{cite news |title=معتقلو الرأي في سوريا |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/0175E8AF-1D2E-4CB2-9473-8EC8528A5224.htm |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=9 March 2011}}</ref> and two days later another special report said that many activists showed their displeasure to the general decree of amnesty did not include political prisoners.<ref>{{cite news |title=ضيق لاستبعاد سياسيين من عفو سوريا |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/C6F6E23B-AC60-4F4F-B3E2-C8F3080DCD2E.htm |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=11 March 2011}}</ref>
