== Protests ==
=== Timeline ===
==== 26–28 January ====
On 26 January 2011, Hasan Ali Akleh from [[Al-Hasakah]] poured gasoline and burned himself in the same way [[Mohamed Bouazizi]] had in Tunis. According to eyewitnesses, the action was "a protest against the [[Syrian government]]".<ref name="Hasan Ali Akleh 1">{{cite web|url=http://www.free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37797 |title=Information on the death of a young man who burned himself in Al Hasakah |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref><ref name="Hasan Ali Akleh 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.metransparent.com/spip.php?page=article&id_article=12661&lang=ar |title=Syrian suicider is "Hasan Ali Akleh". Damascus has banned a demonstration in support of Egypt |publisher=metransparent.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref>

On 28 January 2011, a demonstration was held in [[Ar-Raqqah]] during the evening, in protest against the killing of two soldiers of [[Kurdish people|Kurdish]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37803 |title=Demonstration in Ar-Raqqa, Syria |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref>

==== 2 February ====
On 2 February, [[Human Rights Watch]] reported a group of 20 people in civilian clothing had beaten and dispersed 15 people who had been holding a candlelight vigil at [[Bab Tuma]] in Damascus for [[2011 Egyptian revolution|Egyptian demonstrators]].<ref name=guardian240211>{{cite news| url=http://www.guardian.co.uk/world/2011/feb/24/syria-crackdown-protest-arrests-beatings| title=Syria clamps down on dissent with beatings and arrests| publisher=The Guardian| date=24 February 2011| accessdate=24 February 2011 }}</ref>

==== 4–16 February ====
On 3 February a "Day of rage" was called for in Syria from 4–5 February on [[social media]] sites Facebook and Twitter. Protesters demand reform in the government, however most protests so far have remained outside of Syria and have been small.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.msnbc.msn.com/id/41400687/ns/world_news-mideastn_africa/ |title='Day of rage' protest urged in Syria - World news - Mideast/N. Africa - msnbc.com |publisher=MSNBC |date= |accessdate=2011-02-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aysor.am/en/news/2011/02/03/syrian-facebook-twitter/ |title=“Day of Rage” planned for Syria; protests scheduled for Feb 4-5 - aysor.am - Hot news from Armenia |publisher=aysor.am |date= |accessdate=2011-02-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://freedomist.com/2011/01/30/syran-anger-day-planned-feb-5-2011/|title=Syrian Anger Day Planned Feb 5, 2011|author=William R Collier jr|date=January 30, 2011|publisher=The Freedomist|accessdate=January 31, 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://topsy.com/freedomist.com/2011/01/30/syran-anger-day-planned-feb-5-2011/ |title=Syrian Anger Day Planned Feb 5, 2011 | The Freedomist |publisher=TOPSY |date= |accessdate=2011-02-01}}</ref> The protests were expected to begin on 4 February 2011 as [[social media]] mobilised the people of [[Syria]] for rallies demanding freedom, [[human rights]] and the end to the [[state of emergency]]. Protests were also scheduled for 5 February in front of the parliament in [[Damascus]] and at Syrian embassies internationally.<ref>[http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/02/201122171649677912.html "Calls for weekend protests in Syria"].</ref> ''Al Jazeera'' reported increased security for the planned "Days of rage."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/features/2011/02/201129103121562395.html |title=Syria: 'A kingdom of silence' |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-02-09}}</ref>

The only known action took place February 5, where hundreds of protesters in [[Al-Hasakah]] participated in a mass demonstration, calling for the al-Assad's departure. Syrian authorities have arrested dozens and the demonstration was triggered quickly.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37849 |title=Demonstration on the day of anger in Hasaka and Syrian authorities arrested dozens |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-15}}</ref> Suhair Atassi, who runs the banned [[Jamal al-Atassi|Jamal Atassi Forum]], called for political reforms and the reinstatement of civil rights, as well as an end to the emergency law in place since 1963.<ref>{{cite journal|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/2011/02/201129135657367367.html |title=Q&A: Syrian activist Suhair Atassi |publisher=Al Jazeera English |date=2011-02-09 |accessdate=2011-02-13}}</ref> However, no protests occurred on either date.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.upi.com/Top_News/Special/2011/02/04/Syria-quiet-on-day-of-rage/UPI-58781296853501/ |title=Syria quiet on day of rage |publisher=UPI.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.irishtimes.com/newspaper/world/2011/0205/1224289070429.html |title=Call for Syrian 'day of rage' as planned protests fail to ignite - The Irish Times - Sat, Feb 05, 2011 |publisher=The Irish Times |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Karam |first=Zeina |url=http://www.miamiherald.com/2011/02/05/2052033/syria-escapes-day-of-rage-protests.html |title=Syria escapes 'Day of Rage' protests - World Wires |publisher=MiamiHerald.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-08}}</ref> After the failure of attempts to a "day of rage," ''[[Al Jazeera]]'' called the country "a kingdom of silence" and identified key factors for stability within Syria as being strict security measures, the popularity of President Bashar al-Assad, and fears of potential sectarian violence in the aftermath of a government ouster (akin to neighbouring [[Iraqi insurgency|Iraq]]).<ref>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/features/2011/02/201129103121562395.html |title=Syria: 'A kingdom of silence' |publisher=aljazeera.net |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref>

==== 17 February ====
{{External media
| video1 = {{YouTube|8Cwm5Ck2sSs|Protests in Damscus, Syria, 17 February 2011}}
}}
On 17 February, a spontaneous demonstration took place outside [[Al-Hamidiyah Souq]] in Damascus, in protest at the police beating of a local shop owner. Several men gathered around and blocked the road, while they chanted "The [[Syrian people]] will not be humiliated". An eyewitness estimated the number to more than 1,500 demonstrators. [[Secret police]] officers arrived quickly to the scene along with several government officials and finally Syria's interior minister who dispersed the demonstrators and promised an investigation.<ref name=guardian240211/><ref name="ROBERT MACKEY">{{cite news
| last=  MACKEY
| first= ROBERT
| url=  http://thelede.blogs.nytimes.com/2011/02/17/latest-updates-on-middle-east-protests-4/?partner=rss&emc=rss
| title=  Feb. 17 Updates on Middle East Protest
| publisher=  [[nytimes.com]]
| date=  February 17, 2011
| accessdate= }}</ref> The minister took the shop owner in his car and promised to investigate what had happened.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37926 |title=After the attack on a young man, beating and humiliation: the hundreds of Syrians demonstrating in Harika |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-17}}</ref> A couple of hours later, several video versions were posted on [[YouTube]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qDHLsU-ik_Y |title=Demonstration in Damscus Syria |publisher=[[YouTube]]|date= |accessdate=2011-02-17}}</ref>

==== 22–23 February ====
On 22 February, around 200 people gathered outside the Libyan embassy in [[Damascus]] to protest against the Libyan regime and wanted the ambassador to resign. Despite the peaceful message, there were nearly twice as many secret and uniformed police as protesters. The demonstrators carried placards reading "Freedom for the people" and "Down with [[Gaddafi]]". Everything took a new turn when the protesters started chanting "Traitors are those that beat their people", which created nervousness among the security forces, who immediately asked people to return to their homes. 14 people were arrested and several more beaten by policemen. Some protesters were punched, kicked and beaten with sticks. All present had their identities recorded. Fourteen people were detained but later released.<ref name=guardian240211/>
<ref>{{cite web
| url=  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UNLUCg-TrZU&feature=player_embedded#at=20
| title= الاعتصام امام السفارة الليبية دمشق-22-شباط  Part 5
| publisher=  Youtube
| date=   2011-02-24
| accessdate =
}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
| url=  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLkLqI07erk
| title= الاعتصام امام السفارة الليبية دمشق-22-شباط  Part 8
| publisher=  Youtube
| date=   2011-02-24
| accessdate =
}}</ref>

On 23 February, during a routine session of the [[Parliament of Syria|Syrian parliament]], a member proposed that harsh [[State of emergency|emergency laws]] to be reviewed. The issue was not scheduled for discussion, and it came as a surprise. A pro-government official at the session recalled it with anger. The proposal was rapidly quashed, when the speaker put the proposed review to a vote in the chamber, none of the other 249 MPs supported it.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thenational.ae/news/worldwide/middle-east/syrian-mp-calls-for-review-of-harsh-emergency-laws |title=Syrian MP calls for review of harsh emergency laws |publisher=The National |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>

==== 6–10 March ====
On 6 March, a number of young boys under 15 were arrested in [[Daraa]] because they wrote a slogan of the 2010-2011 Arab uprisings, "the people want to overthrow the regime", on the walls of the city.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/394E1B63-488A-4FF1-BD39-0CD306261FFE.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=سوريا تعتقل شبانا في درعا |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> Libyan [[Anti-Gaddafi forces]] shot down two Syrian war planes in [[Ras Lanuf]], that was later denied by Syrian officials.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.economicvoice.com/libyan-rebels-shoot-down-two-syrian-war-planes/50017136#axzz1FwTDTTfU |title=Libyan rebels shoot down two Syrian war planes |publisher=Economic Voice |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://syria-news.com/readnews.php?sy_seq=129725 |title=مصدر رسمي سوري ينفي مشاركة طيارين سوريين بالقتال في ليبيا |publisher=Syria-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.commentarymagazine.com/2011/03/06/syrian-pilots-shot-down-in-libya/
 |title=Syrian pilots shot down in Libya? |publisher=Commentary Magazine |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> Four brothers that are Syrian opposition activists went missing shortly after passing out flyers in Beirut in front of the Syrian embassy, calling for a demonstration to oppose Syria's government.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.monstersandcritics.com/news/middleeast/news/article_1624021.php/Search-on-for-Syrian-opposition-activists-missing-in-Lebanon |title=Search on for Syrian opposition activists missing in Lebanon |publisher=Middle East News |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref>[[Time (magazine)|Time]] says that the commitment can still be found among the Syrian youth, but what is needed is a starting point.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.time.com/time/world/article/0,8599,2057454,00.html |title=The Youth of Syria: The Rebels Are on Pause |publisher=Time.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref> [[Ribal al-Assad]] says that it is almost time for Syria to be next [[Domino theory|domino]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/opinion/2011/03/20113482455647372.html |title=Is Syria the next domino? |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-06}}</ref>

On 7 March, 13 Syrian political prisoners have gone on [[hunger strike]] to protest against "political detentions and oppression" in Syria. They demanded an end to political arrests, the removal of injustices and the restitution of rights that have been removed from civil and political life<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.jpost.com/Headlines/Article.aspx?id=211153 |title=Syrian prisoners launch hunger strike |publisher=Jerusalem Post |date= |accessdate=2011-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.marxist.com/syria-prisoners-of-conscience-declare-hunger-strike.htm |title=Syria: Prisoners of Conscience in Damascus Central Prison declare hunger strike |publisher=marxist.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-09}}</ref>

On 10 March, dozens of jailed Kurds in Syria, from the ''Yakiti party'' and the ''Democratic Union'', started a hunger strike in solidarity with other activists who have mounted the same action in a prison near Damascus three days earlier.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5j_unWcGEmBcNfMzgi2zu5xlRNDzw?docId=CNG.b10f77bcf3bb312a68aa8c85b50004d3.8e1 |title=Jailed Kurds on Syria hunger strike: rights group |publisher=AFP |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Human Rights Watch]] reacts to the disappearance of Syrian activists in Lebanon four days earlier and fears that [[Lebanon]] is "back to doing Syria's dirty job".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hrw.org/en/news/2011/03/09/lebanon-reveal-fate-disappeared-syrians |title=Lebanon: Reveal Fate of Disappeared Syrians |publisher=HRW |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> Syrian Foreign Ministry stated that Syria is monitoring with high concern "the [[2011 Libyan uprising|tragic developments]] in the brotherly country of [[Libya]]".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=77422 |title=Syria respects Libya's Sovereignty |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> Syrian newspaper [[Al-Watan (Syria)|Al-Watan]] said that  the Syrian government have welcomed the fall of [[Mubarak]]'s regime and are looking forward to a new leadership that does not "cover for Israeli violations".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.syria-today.com/index.php/politics/14482-life-after-mubarak |title=Life After Mubarak |publisher=syria-today.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Iran]]ian official said that they support Syria's right to "restore the occupied [[Golan Heights|Golan]] under its sovereignty".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=77335 |title=Iran support Syria to restore the occupied Golan |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> [[Reform Party of Syria]] said that "al-Assad is sending arms to [[Gaddafi]] to kill his people with".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.worldtribune.com/worldtribune/WTARC/2011/me_libya0257_03_10.asp |title=Syrian pilots said to be flying Libyan fighter jets |publisher=World Tribune |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref>

==== 12–13 March ====
On 12 March, thousands of Syrian Kurds in [[al-Qamishli]] and [[al-Hasakah]] protested on the day of Kurdish martyr, an annual event since 2004, when many Syrian kurds died in anti-government demonstrations.<ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة في الجزيرة السورية 12 اذار 2011 |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7TXnYz5OsI8 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=حفلة تأبين شهداء إنتفاضة قامشلو  |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MWKh8C5Z6ps |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=الكورد السوريون يحييون ذكرى انتفاضتهم السابعة بايقاد الشموع اجلالاً و اكراماُ لارواح الشهداء |author=soparo.com |url=http://ar.soparo.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=17803%3A2011-03-11-23-36-17&catid=34%3A2008-05-23-23-58-43&Itemid=192 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> 

[[Al Jazeera]] reported that a civilian vessel, loaded with weapons and ammunition and 500 [[SUV]]s had sailed from the port of [[Tartus]] in Syria towards [[Tripoli]] in Libya, in order to supply [[Military of Libya|Gaddafi forces]]. The reporter said that the Syrian battalion fighting alongside [[Gaddafi]] against the [[Anti-Gaddafi forces|Libyan rebels]].<ref>{{cite news |title=النظام الاسدي الفاسد يقتل الشعب الليبي باسلحته |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j0ZfsdCXFJA |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=دعم النظام السوري لسفاح ليبيا |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qE6C7LNCRFQ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref> That was also confirmed with an interview with Libyan politician Ibrahim Jibreel.<ref>{{cite news |title=الجزيرة ـ إبراهيم جبريل والتواطؤ السوري مع نظام القذافي |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZaPyJhLIVdA&feature=player_embedded#at=387
 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref>

On 13 March, Kamal Hussein Cheikho, a Kurdish member of the Committee for the Defence of Democratic Liberties and Human Rights in Syria (CDF), was released on bail of 500 Syrian pounds ($10). He is still facing charges for allegedly publishing material harmful to the country.<ref name="OMCT">{{cite news |title=Syrian Arab Republic: Assault and arrest of dozens of family members of human rights defenders and prisoners of conscience |author=OMCT |url=http://www.omct.org/human-rights-defenders/urgent-interventions/syrian-arab-republic/2011/03/d21153/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syria blogger arrest condemned |author=http://mybroadband.co.za |url=http://mybroadband.co.za/news/internet/18663-Syria-blogger-arrest-condemned.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=24 February 2011}}</ref><ref name="ahram">{{cite news |title=Arrests as Syria sees second day of rare protests |author=ahram.org.eg |url=http://english.ahram.org.eg/NewsContent/2/8/7879/World/Region/Arrests-as-Syria-sees-second-day-of-rare-protests.aspx |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 15 March ====
<!-- Deleted image removed: [[File:syria protest.jpg|thumb|A group of supporters of Bashar al-Assad, right, and some opposed to his regime, left, outside the Syrian embassy in Cairo, Egypt]]  -->Simultaneous demonstrations took place in major cities across Syria. Thousands of protestors gathered in [[al-Hasakah]], [[Daraa]], [[Deir ez-Zor]], and [[Hama]]. There were some clashes with security, according to reports from dissident groups. In [[Damascus]], a smaller group of 200 men grew spontaneously to about 1,500 men. Damascus has not seen such protests since the 1980s. The official Facebook page called "Syrian Revolution 2011" shown pictures of supportive demonstrations in [[Cairo]], [[Nicosia]], [[Helsinki]], [[Istanbul]] and [[Berlin]]. There were also unconfirmed news that supporters of Libyan descent stormed into the Syrian Embassy in [[Paris]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Fresh Protests Erupt in Syria |author=theepochtimes.com |url=http://www.theepochtimes.com/n2/content/view/53074/ |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=السوريون يكسرون جدار الخوف بالتظاهر في قلب دمشق |author=Youtube/Al Arabiya |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rRHRxUxRbxo |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=ردّدوا هتافات تدعو لمحاربة الفساد وفتح باب الحريات |author=Al Arabiya |url=http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/03/15/141661.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=الاف السوريين يثورون في قلب دمشق و المحافظات مطالبين بالحرية |author=sawtbeirut.com |url=http://www.sawtbeirut.com/news-in-arabic/world-now/16474-syria-revolution-2011-march-15.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة شباب الثورة في تركيا |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7AbjW0Kuhl8 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

Another recently released political, Suhair Atassi,  became an unofficial spokesperson for the "Syrian revolution", when she was interviewed by dozens of Arab and international media channels regarding the protests.  There were reports of 6 arrested in Damascus.<ref>{{cite news |title=آخر اخبار انتفاضة سورية من فضائية الجزيرة |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sMNBN1PhjzY |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=تقرير قناه ال BBC للانتفاضه السوريه |author=Youtube/BBC |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iAUOqfRSEaM |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة أمام السفارة السورية اليوم بالتزامن مع إنطلاق الثورة السورية |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oCA4iSugabU |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة احتجاج في دمشق تطالب بالحريات |author=BBC |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/arabic/middleeast/2011/03/110315_syria_protest.shtml |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref> Atassi paid tribute to "the Syrian people who took the initiative ahead of the opposition," recalling the popular uprisings that shook [[Tunisian Revolution|Tunisia]] and [[2011 Egyptian revolution|Egypt]]<ref name="ahram" /> After the first day of the protests there were reports about approximately 3000 arrests and a few "[[martyrs]]", but there are no official figures on the number of deaths.<ref>{{cite news |title=معلومات عن سقوط شهداء في تظاهرات الثلاثاء في سوريا |author=sawtbeirut.com |url=http://sawtbeirut.com/news-in-arabic/world-now/16483-2011-03-16-18-33-05.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 16 March ====
Syrian authorities forcibly dispersed a crowd composed of 200 demonstrators in front of the Syrian Interior Ministry. [[al-Arabiya]] reported that protesters were a mix of activists and jurists, writers, journalists, young academics and family members to people detained in Syrian prisons.<ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian Security Forces Break Up Damascus Protest |author=voanews.com |url=http://www.voanews.com/english/news/middle-east/Syrian-Security-Forces-Break-Up-Damascus-Protest-118085199.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Rare public protest quashed in Syria |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011316131236735771.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian security forces disperse Damascus demo |author=AFP |url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5hN_8F5MkEvOPFuMqr-9HStxVy3iw?docId=CNG.e314ba0dc0a9061349e6fe1072be714a.5f1 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> Several security officers managed to infiltrate themselves in demonstrations at different places and started shouting slogans declaring love and loyalty to President Bashar al-Assad.<ref>{{cite news |title=بلطجية بشار |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s0NcdFYFR0Q |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=بلطجية بشار يفرقونا ويهتفون باسمه |author=Facebook Video |url=http://www.facebook.com/video/video.php?v=110837738996912&oid=420796315726&comments |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> The security forces arrested a number of protestors, [[Al Jazeera]] reported 25,<ref>{{cite news |title=مظاهرة أمام القنصلية السورية في اسطنبول |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CyXg8Hm8Hjs |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> while [[Al Arabiya]] said 32<ref>{{cite news |title= أسماء الناشطين المعتقلين من قبل قوات الأمن السورية |author=Youtube/Al Arabiya |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OJCttMTen2w |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> including activist and lawyer [[Suhair Atassi]] and Kamal Cheikho, an activist who was released two days earlier.<ref>{{cite news |title=أهالي سجناء وناشطون حقوقيون شاركوا في التجمع |author=Al Arabiya |url=http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/03/16/141801.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=اعتقالات وتفريق مظاهرة في دمشق |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/04E7BD6C-CA0E-4597-A298-0494DD715056.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=سورية: مظاهرة امام وزارة الداخلية في العاصمة دمشق |author=BBC |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/arabic/middleeast/2011/03/110316_syria_prisoners_families.shtml |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> [[World Organisation Against Torture]] published list of arrests and demanded immediate release of them.<ref name="OMCT" />

''Mohamed al-Ali'', a spokesman for the Syrian Interior Authority denied that any demonstrations have happened in Syria and that the Facebook-campaign has been proved unsuccessful. According to the spokesperson, the "claimed protests" consisted of a bunch of people who were "hiding" among the already packed souq and tried to make it look like a demonstration.<ref>{{cite news |title=تصريح العميد العلي عن مظاهرة وزارة الداخلية |author=Youtube/Al Jazeera |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WevqUv1gGmw |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=16 March 2011}}</ref> In another statement, he went further by saying that the demonstration which was outside the Interior Authority's building was actually in support of President Bashar al-Assad.<ref>{{cite news |title=وزارة الداخلية السورية - المظاهرات كانت لتأييد الرئيس |author=Youtube/BBC |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DXU4ascw1G0
 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=15 March 2011}}</ref>

==== 18 March ====
The most serious unrest to take place in Syria for decades erupted.<ref name=aljaz18311>{{cite news |title= Clashes erupt at protests in Syria |author=Aljazeera |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/201131817214964640.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=18 March 2011}}</ref> After online calls for a "Friday of dignity", thousands of protesters demanding an end to alleged government corruption took to the streets of cities across Syria after Friday prayers.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oC55uPBKYqU |title=Violence flares at Syrian protest |publisher=Youtube/Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref> The protesters were met with a violent crackdown orchestrated by state security forces. The protesters chanted  "God, Syria, Freedom" and anti-corruption slogans.<ref name=bbc18311>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12791738|title=Middle East unrest: Two killed at protest in Syria |publisher=BBC News|date=18 March 2011 |accessdate=18 March 2011}}</ref>

Amateur video footage posted on [[YouTube]] and [[Twitter]] showed large groups of protesters in several cities, such as [[Damascus]], [[Daraa]], [[Homs]], [[Baniyas]], [[al-Qamishli]] and [[Deir ez-Zor]].<ref name= "telegraph110318">{{cite web|url= http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/middleeast/syria/8391438/Syria-four-dead-in-rare-demonstrations.html |title=Syria: 'four dead' in rare demonstrations |publisher=The Telegraph |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref><ref name="ap110318">{{cite web|url= http://www.google.com/hostednews/ap/article/ALeqM5iFVmeKhYv0amn9vzUTAcH4HeEz4Q?docId=3b597c45357b466eae7fcd1d6f4d433d |title= 3 protesters killed in Syria, activist says |publisher=Associated Press |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= http://www.ibtimes.com/articles/124507/20110318/syria.htm |title = Anti-government protests slowly erupting in Syria |publisher= IB Times |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref> In [[Damascus]], civilian dressed Syrian security forces broke up protests outside the [[Umayyad Mosque]], dragging away at least two activists.<ref name=autogenerated1>{{cite web|url=http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/middleeast/syria/8391438/Syria-four-dead-in-rare-demonstrations.html | title = Syria: 'four dead' in rare demonstrations | publisher = The Telegraph |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref> In the southern city of [[Daraa]], people chanted against [[Rami Makhlouf]], the cousin of al-Assad. The regime responded with helicopters and water cannons. There were unconfirmed reports that four protesters had been killed and hundreds injured.<ref name =aljaz18311/><ref name=bbc18311 /> A resident said that Syrian security forces have killed three protesters in Daraa.<ref name=aljaz18311/> The Syrian Government claimed that unnamed "infiltrators" had been attempting to cause chaos.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://af.reuters.com/article/energyOilNews/idAFLDE72H1U520110318?sp=true | title = Syrian forces kill three protesters in southern city |publisher=Reuters |date= |accessdate=2011-03-18}}</ref>

==== 19 March ====
Syrian security forces fired tear gas to disperse crowds in [[Daraa]] following the funeral of two anti-government protesters killed by security forces on the previous day.<ref name=bbc19311>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12796020| title = Syria unrest: Tear gas fired at Deraa funeral | publisher =BBC News|date=19 March 2011 |accessdate=19 March 2011 }}</ref> The crowds had been shouting "God, Syria, freedom" before the security forces intervened.<ref name=bbc19311/> Witnesses said that the gas used appeared to be more toxic than ordinary tear gas.<ref name=nyt19311>{{cite web|url=http://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/20/world/middleeast/20syria.html?_r=1&hp| title = Syrian Police Attack Mourners| publisher =The New York Times|date=19 March 2011 |accessdate=19 March 2011 }}</ref> The Syrian League for Human Rights reported that 10 women who had been detained on 16 March following a rally outside the interior ministry had begun a hunger strike.<ref name=afp19311/>

==== 20 March ====
Thousands took to the streets in the city of Daraa for a third day, shouting slogans against the country's emergency law. One person was killed and scores injured as security forces opened fire on protesters.<ref name="AJSyria20March">{{cite news |title=Syria to free child prisoners |author=[[Al-Jazeera English]] |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011320113138901721.html |newspaper= |date=20 March 2011 |accessdate=20 March 2011}}</ref> The courthouse, the Baath party headquarters in the city, and Rami Makhlouf's [[Syriatel]] building were all set on fire.<ref>[http://www.theglobeandmail.com/news/world/africa-mideast/syrian-protesters-set-fire-to-ruling-partys-headquarters-palace-of-justice/article1948865/ Syrian protesters set fire to ruling party’s headquarters, Palace of Justice - The Globe and Mail<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref>

==== 21 March ====
Protests started to spread further across the country.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://af.reuters.com/article/worldNews/idAFTRE72K6MP20110321?pageNumber=1&virtualBrandChannel=0| title = Allies desert Yemeni leader, Syria protests spread| publisher =Reuters|date=21 March 2011 |accessdate=21 March 2011 }}</ref> Thousands of people took to the streets in Daraa and troops were sent to the city.<ref name=irtimes22311>{{cite web|url=http://www.irishtimes.com/newspaper/world/2011/0322/1224292773018.html| title = US condemns Syria crackdown| publisher =Irish Times|date=22 March 2011 |accessdate=22 March 2011 }}</ref> Hundreds of people protested in Jassem and there were reports of protests in Aleppo, Der Al-Zour, Banias, Homs and Hama.<ref name=irtimes22311/><ref>{{cite news|title=Protests spread to southern Syrian town Jassem|newspaper=Ya Libnan|date=21 March 2011|url=http://www.yalibnan.com/2011/03/21/protests-spread-to-southern-syrian-town-jassem/|accessdate=21 March 2011}}</ref>

It was reported that an 11-year-old boy had died of wounds suffered when Syrian security forces dispersed a protest rally in Daraa,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5ipLl3yeb_p-BMEyqG7WM2O9PYSbg?docId=CNG.e623321b6a9f7efb561fd470f40817bf.9d1| title = US condemns Syria crackdown| publisher =AFP|date=21 March 2011 |accessdate=21 March 2011 }}</ref> and meanwhile, the Beirut-based [[al Akhbar]] newspaper accused Lebonese caretaker Prime Minister [[Saad Hariri]]'s Future Movement of paying the Syrian protesters something that PM immediately denied.<ref>[http://www1.albawaba.com/main-headlines/syrian-army-deploys-around-daraa-lebanon-pm-accused-financing-riots-syria Syrian army deploys around Daraa as Lebanon PM accused of financing riots in Syria | Al Bawaba<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref>

==== 22 March ====
There were protests in Daraa, Jassem, [[Nawa, Syria|Nawa]] and [[Al-Sanamayn|Sanamayn]].<ref name=aljaz22311>{{cite web|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011322155925612161.html| title =UN calls for Syria probe as hundreds protest  | publisher =Al Jazeera|date=22 March 2011 |accessdate=22 March 2011 }}</ref><ref name=cnn22311>{{cite web|url=http://edition.cnn.com/2011/WORLD/meast/03/22/syria.protests/?hpt=T2| title = Syria arrests opposition leader as protests continue| publisher =CNN|date=22 March 2011 |accessdate=22 March 2011 }}</ref> There were also reports of protests in Inkhil and rural areas around Damascus.<ref name=guardian22311>{{cite web|url=http://www.guardian.co.uk/world/2011/mar/22/syrian-protests-troops-kill-deraa| title = Syrian protests spread after troops kill at least five| publisher =The Guardian|date=22 March 2011 |accessdate=22 March 2011 }}</ref> In Daraa, gunfire and tear gas was reported near the Omari mosque, a major gathering spot for protesters.<ref>{{cite web | url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12827542 | title=Syria unrest: 'Gunfire heard' as Deraa protests go on | accessdate=23 March 2011 | date=22 March 2011 | publisher=BBC News Online | archiveurl=http://www.webcitation.org/5xO6FGBue | archivedate=22 March 2011}}</ref>

An AFP photographer and cameraman were beaten by security forces in Daraa and had their equipment seized.<ref name=aljaz22311/>

[[Navi Pillay]], the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights, called for an investigation into the deaths of six protesters who had been killed by Syrian security forces in March.<ref name=aljaz22311/>

====23 March====
{{wikinews|More than a dozen reported dead after Syrian protests}}
There were reports that at least 15 protesters had been killed by security forces in southern Syria.<ref name="cst23311">{{cite web|url=http://www.suntimes.com/news/world/4472184-418/15-killed-in-clashes-in-southern-syria.html|title=15 killed in clashes in southern Syria |publisher=''Chicago Sun=Times''|date=23 March 2011 |accessdate=23 March 2011}}</ref> At least six people were killed by security forces near Al-Omari mosque in Daraa, including a doctor and a paramedic.<ref name="cst23311"/><ref name=bloom23311>{{cite web|url=http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2011-03-23/four-people-killed-in-renewed-unrest-in-southern-syrian-town.html| title =Four People Killed in Renewed Unrest in Southern Syrian Town| publisher =Bloomberg|date=23 March 2011 |accessdate=23 March 2011 }}</ref> Witnesses reported that a 12-year-old girl had been killed by security forces near the mosque.<ref name="cst23311"/>

Mobile phone connections to Daraa were cut during the day and checkpoints set up throughout the city manned by soldiers.<ref name="cst23311"/> Security forces were also preventing ambulances from entering the city centre, where the mosque is located. Demonstrations were under way in [[Aleppo]] as well,  in support of protesters in Daraa.<ref>{{cite news |title=Syrian forces 'open fire on protesters' |author=[[Al-Jazeera English]] |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/20113231127592364.html |newspaper= |date=23 March 2011 |accessdate=23 March 2011}}</ref>

On the evening of the 23rd there were reports that Syrian security forces had opened fire on hundreds of young protesters who had been marching towards Daraa.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12843905| title =Syria unrest: Troops 'fire on youths in Deraa'| publisher =BBC News|date=23 March 2011 |accessdate=23 March 2011 }}</ref>

====24 March====
Around 20,000 protesters marched at the funerals of nine protesters killed by security forces in Daraa.<ref>{{cite news|title=Anger in Syria over crackdown|author=|newspaper=Al Jazeera|date=24 Mar 2011|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/201132475046579830.html|accessdate=24 Mar 2011}}</ref>

==== 25 March ====
There were reports that at least 20 people were killed in protests in Daraa involving over 100,000 people.<ref name=cnn25311>{{cite news|title=In Syrian flashpoint town, more deaths reported|publisher=CNN|date=25 March 2011|url=http://edition.cnn.com/2011/WORLD/meast/03/25/syria.unrest/index.html?hpt=T1|accessdate=25 Mar 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|title=Security Forces 'Kill 20 Protesters' In Syria |publisher=Sky News|date=25 March 2011|url=http://news.sky.com/skynews/Home/World-News/Video-Syria-Security-Forces-Open-Fire-On-Protesters-Killing-20-People-According-To-Reports/Article/201103415960221?lpos=World_News_Top_Stories_Header_3&lid=ARTICLE_15960221_Video%2C_Syria%3A_Security_Fzorces_Open_Fire_On_Protesters_Killing_20_People%2C_According_To_Reports|accessdate=25 Mar 2011}}</ref> A witness said that thousands of people chanted against the president's brother: "Maher you coward. Send your troops to liberate the Golan,"<ref>{{cite news|title=Syrian protesters denounce Assad's brother|newspaper=Reuters|date=Mar 25, 2011|url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/25/syria-protest-assad-idUSLDE72O17Y20110325|accessdate=Mar 25, 2011}}</ref> A statue of [[Hafez al-Assad]] was dismantled and set on fire.<ref name=AlJazeera260311>{{cite news|title=Syrian protesters target Baath Party offices|newspaper=Al Jazeera|date=26 Mar 2011|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/201132616546326475.html|accessdate=26 Mar 2011}}</ref> The governors home was also set on fire.<ref name=AlJazeera260311/>

There were also reports of protests in Damascus, [[Deir ez-Zor]], [[Homs]], [[Latakia]] and [[Ar-Raqqah|Raqqa]].<ref name=cnn25311/><ref name=Haaretz250311>{{cite news|title=At least 23 said killed as protesters in Syria clash with security forces|newspaper=Haaretz|date=25 March 2011|url=http://www.haaretz.com/news/international/at-least-23-said-killed-as-protesters-in-syria-clash-with-security-forces-1.351815|accessdate=26 March 2011}}</ref> There were reports that one demonstrator had been shot dead by security forces in Latakia and another had been killed in Homs.<ref>{{cite news|title=SYRIA: Government forces open fire on protesters|publisher=Los Angeles Times|date=24 March 2011|url=http://latimesblogs.latimes.com/babylonbeyond/2011/03/syria-government-forces-open-fire-on-protesters.html|accessdate=24 Mar 2011}}</ref> There were reports that dozens of protesters had died across the country.<ref>{{cite news|title=SYRIA: Government forces open fire on protesters|publisher=LA Times|date=25 March 2011|url=http://latimesblogs.latimes.com/babylonbeyond/2011/03/syria-government-forces-open-fire-on-protesters.html|accessdate=25 Mar 2011}}</ref>

A witness said that in Sanamayn, security forces killed 20 people.<ref>{{cite news|title=Deaths as Syrian forces fire on protesters|newspaper=AL Jazeera|date=26 Mar 2011|url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011325145817688433.html|accessdate=26 Mar 2011}}</ref><ref name=Haaretz250311/> The official Syrian news agency reported that an "armed gang" had attacked army headquarters there, that "resulted in the deaths of several attackers."<ref name=SANA250311>{{cite news|title=Armed Gang Attacks People's Army Headquarter, Several Attackers killed… Another Armed Gang Attacks Officers Club in Homs and Opens Fire, Martyring a Citizen|author=M.Nassr/Ghossoun/H. Sabbagh|newspaper=Syrian Arab News Agency|date=25 March 2011|url=http://www.sana.sy/eng/21/2011/03/25/338516.htm|accessdate=26 March 2011}}</ref> The death of a civilian in Homs was blamed on an "armed group".<ref name=SANA250311/>

Most chants called for solidarity with Daraa and the people killed there, for freedom, and against regime corruption.<ref>{{cite news|title=Violence erupts in Syria; troops open fire on anti-protestors|publisher=New York Daily Times|date=25 March 2011|url=http://www.nydailynews.com/news/world/2011/03/25/2011-03-25_violence_erupts_in_syria_troops_open_fire_on_antiprotestors_.html?r=news|accessdate=25 Mar 2011}}</ref> 

In Tafas, 3 protesters were killed by security forces.<ref name=AlJazeera260311/>

The Sunni cleric Sheikh [[Yusuf al-Qaradawi]] gave a sermon in Qatar in which he stated: "Today the train of revolution has arrived at a station that it was destined to reach, the Syrian station. It isn't possible for Syria to detach itself from the history of the Arab nation."<ref name=wsj25311/>

==== 26 March ====
In the cities of Latakia and Tafas, Baath party buildings and police stations were set on fire.<ref name=AlJazeera260311/> Armed gangs were blamed by the authorities on attempting to destabilise the country.<ref>[http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/mobile/world-middle-east-12873053 BBC news:Syria unrest]</ref> The Syrian News Agency (SANA) reported that an armed group seized rooftops in many areas in Latakia, opening fire on citizens and security forces personnel, though the news agency is controlled by the government.<ref>[http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=79055 DP-news:Damascus was Quite on Saturday]</ref> Two people were killed, and thousands more protested in Daraa.<ref name=AlJazeera260311/>
==== 27 March ====
Syrian officials reported that 12 people were killed in [[Latakia]].
<ref>[http://www.guardian.co.uk/world/2011/mar/27/report-12-killed-syrian-port-city]</ref>
The Syrian President decided to lift the emergency law.<ref>[http://www.english.rfi.fr/middle-east/20110326-syria-lifts-emergency-rule-tensions-remain-high]</ref><ref>http://www.nowlebanon.com/NewsArticleDetails.aspx?ID=254900</ref>

==Reactions==
===Domestic===
====Arrests and convictions====

Days before protests planned for 5 February, Syrian authorities arrested several political activists, such as businessman Ghassan al-Najar, leader of the Islamic Democratic movement,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.elaph.com/Web/news/2011/2/629499.html |title=Arrest of leader of the Islamic Democratic movement in Syria |publisher=elaph.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37876 |title=Transfer of activist Ghassan Najar to Adra prison after being referred for trial |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> the writer Ali al-Abdallah,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37861 |title=Start of the trial exhibits and the Syrian writer Ali Abdullah |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> Abbas Abbas, from the [[Communist Party of Labour]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37788 |title=Jailed prominent Syrian opposition for seven and a half years |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref> and several other political personalities of Kurdish backgrounds, such as Adnan Mustafa.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://free-syria.com/loadarticle.php?articleid=37802 |title=Syrian authorities detain national identity Adnan Mustafa Abu Ammar |publisher=free-syria.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-12}}</ref>

On 14 February, blogger and student [[Tal al-Mallohi]] was convicted of spying for the United States and sentenced to five years in prison. Washington denied these allegations and asked for al-Mallohi's immediate release. On 15 February under pressure from human rights organizations, the Syrian government released ''Ghassan al-Najjar'', a "veteran Islamist activist" after he went on hunger strike following his arrest for calling for mass protests.<ref>{{cite news | url = http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/02/16/us-protests-idUSTRE71F41K20110216?pageNumber=2 | title = Egypt-inspired protests gain pace across region | first = Paul | last = Taylor | date = 2011-02-16 | publisher = Reuters }}</ref> On 16 February regime critic and director of the Organisation for Democracy and Freedom in Syria (ODFS) [[Ribal al-Assad]], son of [[Rifaat al-Assad]] and cousin to Syrian President Bashar al-Assad, held a press conference in London, in which he made it clear that he "does not want to see a Syrian revolution, but a peaceful change of power".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.elaph.com/Web/news/2011/2/632409.html |title=Opposition leader Ribal Al-Assad is optimistic to changes in Syria |publisher=elaph.com |date= |accessdate=2011-02-16}}</ref>

On 22 March Syrian authorities arrested Loay Hussein, a prominent human rights campaigner.<ref name=cnn22311/>

On 25 March there were reports of mass arrests and detentions of protesters taking place.<ref name=wsj25311>{{cite web|url=http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052748704517404576222350109783770.html| title = Troops Open Fire on Syrian Protesters | publisher =The Wall Street Journal|date=25 March 2011 |accessdate=25 March 2011 }}</ref>

====Censorship====
On 29 January 2011, ''[[Al Arabiya]]'' reported that Internet connections had been cut off; however, the reported was quickly denied by official sources.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dp-news.com/pages/detail.aspx?l=2&articleId=72236 |title=Internet is Available across Syria |publisher=dp-news.com |date= |accessdate=2011-01-30}}</ref>

On 5 February, Internet services were said to have been curbed, although ''[[Facebook]]'' and ''[[YouTube]]'' were reported to have been restored on 8 February.<ref name="JENNIFER PRESTON">{{cite news
| last=  PRESTON
| first=  JENNIFER
| url=  http://www.nytimes.com/2011/02/10/world/middleeast/10syria.html?_r=1
| title=  Syria Restores Access to Facebook and YouTube
| publisher=  [[The New York Times]]
| date=  9 February 2011
| accessdate= }}</ref> Suggestions were made that easing the ban could be a way to track activists.<ref>{{cite web|author=Jillian York |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/indepth/opinion/2011/02/2011212122746819907.html |title=Unblocking Syria's social media |publisher=Al Jazeera English |date= |accessdate=2011-02-13}}</ref>

====Concessions====
On 19 March, Assad issued a decree shortening the length of mandatory army [[conscription]] from 21 to 18 months.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/19/us-syria-idUSTRE72I22020110319?pageNumber=2| title = Syrian mourners call for revolt, forces fire tear gas| publisher =Reuters|date=19 March 2011 |accessdate=19 March 2011 }}</ref>

On 20 March, the Syrian government announced that it would release the 15 children who had been arrested on March 6th for writing pro-democracy graffiti.<ref name="AJSyria20March"/>

On 22 March, the Governor of Deraa [[Faisal Kalthoum]] was dismissed.<ref name=wsj23311/>

On 24 March, the president's media adviser, [[Buthaina Shaaban]], said that the government will be "studying the possibility of lifting the emergency law and licensing political parties". The Syrian government also announced a cut in personal taxation rates, an increase in public sector salaries of 1,500 Syrian pounds ($32.60 US) a month and pledges to increase press freedom, create more employment opportunities and reduce corruption.<ref name=cnn25311/><ref>{{cite news |title=Syria pledges reforms after protests |author= |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011324164740741279.html |newspaper= |date=24 March 2011 |accessdate=24 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Syria unrest: Government pledges political reforms |author=[[BBC]] |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12853634 |newspaper= |date=24 March 2011} |accessdate=24 March 2011}}</ref>

On March 26, Syrian authorities freed more than 200 political prisoners - 70 according to other sources -, mostly Islamists, being held in Saidnaya prison.<ref name="BBCSyria26Mar">{{cite news |title=Syria turmoil: Political inmates 'freed' after protests |author=[[BBC]] |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12870679 |newspaper= |date=26 March 2011 |accessdate=26 March 2011}}</ref>

On 27 March, Bouthaina Shaaban confirmed that the emergency law will be lifted, but failed to give a timetable.<ref>{{cite news |title=Syria 'to lift emergency law' |author=[[Al-Jazeera English]] |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/201132711553545999.html |newspaper= |date=27 March 2011} |accessdate=27 March 2011}}</ref>

====Propaganda====
On 22 March there were reports in ''[[The Guardian]]'' that the Syrian authorities had been organising pro-Assad rallies and distributing propaganda blaming the unrest on saboteurs and infiltrators.<ref name=guardian22311/>

On 25 March, pro-Assad rallies were held in Damascus.<ref name=wsj25311/>

====Other====
On 8 March, [[Syrian Arab News Agency|SANA]], the official Syrian news agency, published an article on its website titled "President [[Bashar al-Assad|al-Assad]] issued a decree provides for a legislative grant amnesty for political crimes committed before the date of 2011-03-08". Three hours later, the publication was removed.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://all4syria.info/content/view/40436/96/ |title=سيادة الرئيس يصدر عفواً عن الجرائم السياسية ثم يسحب بعد ثلاث ساعات ؟ |publisher=All4Syria |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}{{ar}}</ref> Hours later, Syrian authorities released [[Haitham al-Maleh]], an 80-year-old former judge, one of al-Assad's most outspoken critics, under an amnesty marking the anniversary of the [[1963 Syrian coup d'état|1963 coup]] which brought the [[Baath Party]] to power.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-12679902 |title=Syrian activist [[Haitham al-Maleh]] freed under amnesty |publisher=BBC News |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/08/us-syria-rights-idUSTRE7274LC20110308 |title=Syria frees 80-year-old former judge in amnesty |publisher=Reuters |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref> Twelve Syrian [[human rights]] organisations called on the government to scrap the [[state of emergency]] which has stifled civil society for almost 50 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5jVJSMH9FyXYejUJ-MoOUniMK6CBg?docId=CNG.08dc880232fd4c049d2f1a10f4641409.cf1 |title=12 rights groups in Syria demand end to emergency |publisher=AFP |date= |accessdate=2011-03-08}}</ref>

On 12 March, newly released regime critic [[Haitham al-Maleh]] announced in a YouTube video his support and assistance to the Syrian youth who are behind the new wave of protests and hoped that he will soon see democracy in Syria.<ref>{{cite news |title=هيثم المالح .. يوجه رسالة إلى شباب الثورة السورية |author=Youtube |url=http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EVYVtaVKnf0 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=12 March 2011}}</ref>

===International===
====Supranational====
*{{flagicon|European Union}} [[European Union]] - On 22 March [[Catherine Ashton]], the [[High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy]], issued a statement which said that the European Union "strongly condemns the violent repression, including through the use of live ammunition, of peaceful protests in various locations across Syria".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.middle-east-online.com/english/?id=45066|title=EU condemns 'unacceptable' repression in Syria |publisher=Middle East Online |date=22 March 2011|accessdate=22 March 2011}}</ref>
*{{flagicon|United Nations}} [[United Nations]] – On 18 March the [[Secretary-General of the United Nations|UN Secretary General]] [[Ban Ki-moon]] described the use of deadly force against protesters by the Syrian authorities as "unacceptable".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://english.aljazeera.net/news/middleeast/2011/03/2011318231622114396.html |title=UN chief slams Syria's crackdown on protests |publisher=Al Jazeera |date=18 March 2011}}</ref>

====States====
*{{flag|Australia}} – It was reported on March 25, that the Australian foreign minister, [[Kevin Rudd]], had said: "we are deeply sceptical about the official explanations as to what has happened with the various killings which have occurred in Daraa in the southern part of the country. And we call directly on the Syrian Government to exercise restraint in their response to peaceful protest seeking democratic change."<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/pm/content/2011/s3174118.htm|title=Australia advises citizens not to travel to Syria|author=Alexandra kirk|date=March 25 2011|publisher=abc.net.au|accessdate=26 March 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|Canada}} – On March 21 the Canadian Foreign Affairs Minister, [[Lawrence Cannon]] stated: "Canada deplores the multiple deaths and injuries following protests in several Syrian cities over the weekend."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5jj7uSbIAaro1v0fwW4jziIxQ1j9w?docId=CNG.a807bd69f3debaa7a6b4ca2383f9500b.1191|title=Canada condemns violence in Yemen, Bahrain, Syria|publisher=AFP |date=21 March 2011|accessdate=22 March 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|France}} – The Foreign Ministry condemned the violence carried out against demonstrators and called for political prisoners to be freed.<ref>{{cite news |title=France condemns violence against protestors in Syria |author=Lebanon News |url=http://www.nowlebanon.com/NewsArchiveDetails.aspx?ID=251195 |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=17 March 2011}}</ref> On 23 March, Bernard Valero, the French foreign ministry spokesman, called on Syria to carry out immediate political reforms.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/23/us-france-syria-idUSTRE72M39920110323|title=France urges Syria to make immediate political reforms|publisher=Reuters|date=23 March 2011|accessdate=23 March 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|Germany}} – On March 24, the German Foreign Minister, [[Guido Westerwelle]], said: "The violence must end immediately. The Syrian government must make sure that basic human and civil rights, as well as the rule of law, is observed,"<ref>{{cite news|title=Germany urges Syria to halt violence, launch talks|author=Reporting by Brian Rohan, John Irish, Catherine Bremer; Editing by Elizabeth Fullerton|newspaper=Reuters|date=Mar 24, 2011|url=http://www.reuters.com/article/2011/03/24/us-syria-france-germany-idUSTRE72N49O20110324|accessdate=Mar 25, 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|Israel}} – On March 24, the Israeli Minister of Foreign Affairs, [[Avigdor Lieberman]], said: "I think that the same principles, activities the Western world (has taken) in Libya ... I hope to see those regarding the Iranian regime and the Syrian regime."<ref>{{cite news|title=INTERVIEW-West should treat Syria, Iran like Libya-Israel|newspaper=Reuters|date=Mar 24, 2011|url=http://af.reuters.com/article/energyOilNews/idAFLDE72N1AZ20110324|accessdate=Mar 25, 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|Norway}} – On March 24, the Norwegian minister of foreign affairs [[Jonas Gahr Støre]] condemned the violence saying: "Norway urges the authorities of Syria not to use violence against peaceful protesters, to respect the freedom of speech and assembly and to enter into a dialogue with the people about their legitimate demands".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.nrk.no/nyheter/verden/1.7563366 |title=Sykehus tok imot 25 døde demonstranter i Syria|publisher=NRK |date=24 March 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|Turkey}} – On March 21, the Turkish foreign minister, [[Ahmet Davutoğlu]], said: "Syria is on an important threshold. We hope problems between the people and the administration [in Syria] can be handled without trouble."<ref>{{cite news|title=Turkey anxious over protests in Syria|author=SEVİL KÜÇÜKKOŞUM|newspaper=Hürriyet Daily News|date=March 22, 2011|url=http://www.hurriyetdailynews.com/n.php?n=turkey-anxious-over-protests-in-syria-2011-03-22|accessdate=March 23, 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|United Kingdom}} – On March 24, the British Foreign Secretary, [[William Hague]], said: "We call on the government of Syria to respect their people's right to peaceful protest and to take action about their legitimate grievances,"<ref>{{cite news|title='More than 100 killed in Syrian anti-government rallies'|author=Oren Kessler and Reuters|newspaper=The Jerusalem Post|date=Mar 24, 2011|url=http://www.jpost.com/MiddleEast/Article.aspx?ID=213609&R=R1|accessdate=Mar 25, 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|United States}} – President [[Barack Obama]]'s administration condemned the use of violence, stating: “The United States stands for a set of universal rights, including the freedom of expression and assembly, and believes that governments, including the Syrian government, must address the legitimate aspirations of their people."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2011-03-18/obama-administration-condemns-syrian-violence-against-protests.html |title=Obama Administration Condemns Syrian Violence Against Protests |publisher=Bloomberg |date=18 March 2011}}</ref>
*{{flag|Venezuela}} – It was reported on March 26, that the president of Venezuela, [[Hugo Chavez]], had said: "Now some supposed political protest movements have begun (in Syria), a few deaths ... and now they are accusing the president of killing his people and later the Yankees will come to bomb the people to save them,", "How cynical is the new format the empire has invented, to generate violent conflict, generate blood in a country, to later bombard it, intervene and take over its natural resources and convert it into a colony,"<ref>{{cite news|title=Venezuela's Chavez offers supports to Syrian leader amid protests, blames US for unrest|newspaper=theguardian.pe.ca|date=March 26, 2011|url=http://www.theguardian.pe.ca/Canada---World/Society/2011-03-26/article-2369627/Venezuelas-Chavez-offers-supports-to-Syrian-leader-amid-protests,-blames-US-for-unrest/1|accessdate=March 27, 2011}}</ref>

====NGOs====
*[[Amnesty International]] condemned the "violent crackdown against a peaceful protest" by people calling for the release of political prisoners.<ref>{{cite news |title=Amnesty condemns Syrian crackdown on protest |author=Reuters/Yahoo |url=http://uk.news.yahoo.com/22/20110317/tpl-uk-syria-protest-amnesty-81f3b62.html |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=17 March 2011}}</ref>
*[[Human Rights Watch]] stated that the Syrian government has shown "no qualms about shooting dead its own citizens for speaking out." It also said that Syrians have shown "incredible courage in daring to protest publicly against one of the most repressive governments in the region, and they shouldn't have to pay with their lives."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rferl.org/content/rights_groups_condemn_syria/2345012.html |title=Rights Groups Condemn Syria's Violent Crackdown On Protesters |publisher=RFE/RL |date=21 March 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.hrw.org/en/news/2011/03/20/syria-government-crackdown-leads-protester-deaths |title=Syria: Government Crackdown Leads to Protester Deaths |publisher=Human Rights Watch |date=20 March 2011 }}</ref>
====Individuals====
Egyptian [[Islamist]], [[Muslim Brotherhood]] linked scholar [[Yusuf al-Qaradawi]] declared his support for the protests against what he called Syria's "oppresive regime", claiming "atrocities" were committed by it. He called for victory against the ruling Ba'ath party and claimed the army would be the major factor in the revolt. Qaradawi also expressed his support for the no Fly zone put in place by western nations over Libya, saying "The operation in Libya is to protect the civilians from Gaddafi’s tyranny", and slamming the Arab League leader Amr Moussa for criticism of it.<ref>{{cite news|title=Qaradawi condemns ‘atrocities’ against protesters in Syria‎|author=Nour Abuzant|url=http://www.gulf-times.com/site/topics/article.asp?cu_no=2&item_no=424384&version=1&template_id=57&parent_id=56|newspaper=Gulf Times|date=Saturday26/3/2011March, 2011, 02:10 AM Doha Time|accessdate=March 26, 2011}}</ref> Qaradawi said all Arabs should back up the protests in Syria, saying "Today the train of revolution has reached a station that it had to reach: The Syria station", and "It is not possible for Syria to be separated from the history of the Arab community".<ref>{{cite news|title=Protests in Syria's Daraa city, journalists banned|author=|url=http://www.monstersandcritics.com/news/middleeast/news/article_1628710.php/Protests-in-Syria-s-Daraa-city-journalists-banned|newspaper=Deutsche Presse-Agentur|date=Mar 25, 2011, 12:34 GMT|accessdate=March 26, 2011}}</ref> he Muslim Brotherhood assisted in the protests, Islamic clergy called on Sunnis to pour onto the streets throughout Syria and expel the Alawi regime, and Qaradawi called on the Sunnis of Syria to claim their rights from the regime.<ref>{{cite news|title=To survive, Assad must contain majority Sunni unrest before it infects army|author=|url=http://www.debka.com/article/20803/|newspaper=DEBKAfile|date=March 26, 2011, 1:45 PM (GMT+02:00)|accessdate=March 26, 2011}}</ref>

====Media====
Under criticism from Internet activists for failing to acknowledge the Syrian protests, ''[[Al Jazeera]]'' has broadcast special coverage  of the Hama massacre as well as analysis of the largest [[opposition parties]] in Syria which might have great political influence in any change of power: the [[Syrian People's Democratic Party]], [[Muslim Brotherhood#Syria|Muslim Brotherhood]], [[National Salvation Front in Syria|National Salvation Front]], [[Movement for Justice and Development in Syria|Movement for Justice and Development]], [[Reform Party of Syria|Reform Party]], [[Arab Socialist Movement]], [[Arab Socialist Union (Syria)|Arab Socialist Union]], [[Workers Revolutionary Party]], Communist Party of Labour, and others.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/605F9657-EC6C-4754-A38B-989C563CB129.htm?GoogleStatID=9 |title=قوى المعارضة في سوريا |publisher=Al Jazeera |date= |accessdate=2011-03-10}}</ref> On March 9, [[Al Jazeera]] continued its reporting with analysis of opinion detainees in Syria<ref>{{cite news |title=معتقلو الرأي في سوريا |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/0175E8AF-1D2E-4CB2-9473-8EC8528A5224.htm |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=9 March 2011}}</ref> and two days later another special report said that many activists showed their displeasure to the general decree of amnesty did not include political prisoners.<ref>{{cite news |title=ضيق لاستبعاد سياسيين من عفو سوريا |author=Al Jazeera |url=http://www.aljazeera.net/NR/exeres/C6F6E23B-AC60-4F4F-B3E2-C8F3080DCD2E.htm |newspaper= |date= |accessdate=11 March 2011}}</ref>

On 23 March a column was published in ''[[The Daily Telegraph]]'' by [[Con Coughlin]], the newspaper's executive foreign editor, calling for the creation of a  [[no-fly zone]] over Syria to protect innocent protesters.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://blogs.telegraph.co.uk/news/concoughlin/100080948/now-lets-have-a-no-fly-zone-for-syria-and-yemen/| title =Now let's have a no-fly zone for Syria and Yemen| publisher =The Telegraph|date=23 March 2011 |accessdate=23 March 2011 }}</ref>
